A fragmentary record lying closely and engraved in characters of a slightly later
period and therefore probably not connected with the above contains portion of
the imprecatory verses which reads:
• • • • • • • • • •
• • • • • • • • • • nyōyaṁ dharmma
• • • • • • • • • • yōbhavaddhi sarvvānētāṁbhā
• • • • • • • • • • yācatē rāmacaṁdraḥ dāna
• • • • • • • • • • pālanaṁ dānāsvarggamavāpnōti
• • • • • • • • • • paradatta vā yō harēti

inscription.head:
No. 225.
(A.R. No. 527 of 1929-30.)
KURUNĀḌU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the second prākāra of the Sōmanāthēśvara temple.

This damaged record refers to Baṁkidēvarasa who may probably
be the Āḷupa chief and other officials who met in the hiriya-aramane
(big palace) at Maṅgaḷūru. It seems to register a gift, the purport of
which is not however, clear.

If may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to about the 13th
century is dated the cyclic year Durmukhi, Siṁha 27, Thursday. The
tithi corresponds to 1296 A.D., August 24. The weekday, however
was Thursday, falling in the reign period of Baṅkidēva.

inscription.head:
No. 226.
(A.R. No. 338 of 1930-31.)
SUJĒRU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in a field.

This record registers a royal gift of land to god Timirēśvara, on
the occasion when rains set in as a result of prayer during the period
of famine. It states that the king was giving audience in the hall
(mokaśāle) called Bhuvanāśraya of his palace (hiriya-aramane) at
rājadhāni Maṅgalāpura.

The inscription is dated Śaka 1228, Viśvavasu, Siṁha 18,
Sunday corresponding to 1305 A.D., August 15. The Śaka year was
current.

inscription.head:
No. 227.
(A.R. No. 345 of 1931-32.)
HĀVAÑJE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing in Survey No. 49.

This badly damaged record registers a gift of land by the king,
Kotāḷuva-daṇṇāyaka, the pradhānas, and others. It refers to Bemmu-
sēna-bōva, son-in-law of Baṁki-sēnabōva.

It is dated Śaka 1240, Piṅgaḷa, Chaitra śu. 1, Tuesday
corresponding to 1317 A.D., March 14, the weekday, however, being
Monday and the Śaka year current.

l.note: The rest of the

inscription.head:
No. 228.
(A.R. No. 554 of 1929-30.)
UPPUNDA, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near a field called ‘Mañjayyabhaṭṭara-hola’.

This badly damaged record in characters of about the 14th
century is dated the cyclic year Dundubhi, [Jyēshṭha] śu. 15, Monday
probably corresponding to 1322 A.D., May 31, when Vīra Sōyidēva
of the Āḷupa family was ruling.

It seems to registers a gift of land probably by a lady. All
details are lost.

l.ka: • ḷu • ke taṁna kuṭuṁbasahitavāgi • • • • • si koṭṭaḷu •

inscription.head:
No. 229.
(A.R. No. 308 of 1931-32.)
ĀVARŚE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing in ‘Hanubinemalla-gadde’ at the hamlet Kirāḍi.

The record registers a gift of land by mahāpradhāna Siṅgaṇ-
sāhaṇi together with Baṁchana-hegaḍe and another seṭṭi of Kiṟuhāḍe to
Mādhava-kalukura, son of Śiva-kalukura with a stipulation that the
donee had to pay a specified sum of money regularly to god
Kōṭīśvaradēva.

It is dated the cyclic year Rudhirōdgārin, Kanyā 12, Saturday.
These details correspond to 1323 A.D., September 10.

l.ka: ṇa sāhaṇi rudhirōdgā[ri*] saṁvatsarada kaṁnyā māsaṁ ha-
heggaḍe yaḷḷaseṭi tamoḷēkastharāgi sivakalukurana maga mā-

inscription.head:
No. 230.
(A.R. No. 550 of 1929-30.)
PAḌUVARI, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field.

The record is dated Śaka 12[4]6, Raktākshi, Siṁha 17, Wednesday
corresponding to 1324 A.D., August 14, the weekday being, however,
Tuesday.

It records a gift of land, situated in Bayidūru, excluding the
dēvasva belonging to god Baṁkēśvara to god Sōmanātha and another
deity (name lost), by the king and Siṁgaṇṇa-sāhaṇi, grandson of
mahāpradhāna Sōvaṇṇa-sēnabōva• It states that Mallikārjuna-gōpa, son
of • • dādēvi was to protect the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 231.
(A.R. No. 374 of 1930-31.)
PĀDEBEṬṬU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in Dammarguḍḍe, about a mile to the north of the
Subrahmanya temple.

The badly damaged record seems to register a gift of money and
land the king to god Kōṭiśvaradēva. It refers to mahāpradhāna Sōvaṇṇa
and senabōva Singaṇṇa-sāhaṇi.

The inscription is dated Śaka 12[46], Raktākshi, the other details
being lost. The year corresponds to 1324-25 A.D.

l.ka: śrī vīra sōyidē[vāḷu]pēṁdra dēvarasaru vijayarājya mutta-

inscription.head:
No. 232.
(A.R. No. 573 of 1929-30.)
MŪḌA-NIḌAMBŪRU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in the prākāra of the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple at Bannañje,
a suburb.

The record is dated the cyclic year Prabhava, Āshāḍha ba. 3,
Karkāṭaka 9, Tuesday falling in the reign of the king Sōidēva. The
details correspond to 1327 A.D., July 7, Tuesday, f.d.t. ·15.

It registers a gift of forest-land at Baṁniṁje and money to god
Tāḍeguḍeyadēva by the king, mahāpradhāna Siṁgaṇṇa, sāhaṇi Boppaṇṇa,
adhikāri Nāḍapana Koṁḍakala-nāyaka and the ūr.

inscription.head:
No. 233.
(A.R. No. 527 of 1928-29.)
VARĀṄGA, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in the southern verandah of the outer prākāra of
Nēmīśvara basti.

The badly damaged record refers to the rule of Śrī Sōmēśvararāya
of Sōma-vaṁśa, bearing the epithets Paṇḍita-pāṇḍya, Pāṇḍya-dhanaṁjaya,
Arirāya-Basavaśaṅkara etc. The king is identical with Sōyidēva.

It is dated Śaka 1[25]4, Āṅgira śu. 10, Thursday, the name of
the month being lost. The year corresponds to 1332-33 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 234.
(A.R. No. 461 of 1928-29.)
KUḌUPU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in the outer prākāra of the Ananta Padmanābhasvāmin
temple.

The record is dated the cyclic year Bhāva, Kuṁbha 22,
Wednesday. On the 14th century and the date equated to 1335 A.D.,
February 15.

The record which is much damaged refers to the rule of Vīra-
Sōyirāya of the Āḷupa dynasty bearing the titles Pānḍya-Chakravarti,
Basavaśaṅkara and Rāyagajāṁkuśa, and seems to registers a gift of
money-income. Mentions Mayinda-heggaḍe and the village Kuḍupa.

l.ka: golavana baṁdu gasādi • yalli miri baṁ-
vūriṁda vobaru mitava koṭṭaḍe daḍagaḷa • do

inscription.head:
No. 235.
(A.R. No. 556 of 1929-30.)
UPPUNDA, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field called ‘Beḍagalkaṭṭe hola’.

The badly damaged record registers a gift of land in the villages
Uppugunda and Hesarūru to Nāraṇa-kāraṁtaḍigaḷu. All other details are
lost.

l.note:
A few lines engraved at
badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 236.
(A.R. No. 596 of 1929-30.)
HANDĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field called ‘Govindaseṭṭi-gadde’.

This damaged record is dated Śaka 1261 Bahudhānya, other
details being lost. The year corresponds to 1338-39 A.D., the Śaka year
being current.

It registers a gift of 24 mūḍe of land situated in Brahmūru and
Haṅdāḍi, to god Sōmanāthā-dēvarasa.

l.ka: ḍḍolagaṁ goṭṭi[rdda kā]ladalu samasta pradhānaru muṁtāgi
sanada krama[veṁteṁdare] ·ā sōmanātha dēvara dēvasva brahma

inscription.head:
No. 237.
(A.R. No. 496 of 1928-29.)
NĪLĀVARA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 7) set up in the prākāra of Mahishāsuramardini temple.

The badly damaged record is dated Śaka 12[6]7, Tāraṇa,
Mārggaśira ba. 3, Vṛiśchika 27, Thursday. The given details, bearing
the week-day which was Tuesday, correspond to 1344 A.D., November
23, the Śaka year being current.

It seems to register a gift of land to the goddess Bhagavatī of
Niruvāra by the king.

ka.b: tsarada mārggaśira ba 3 vṛścika 27 guruvā[ra*] śrīvīra pāṁ • •

l.ka: yō bhūyō yācatē rāmacaṁdra | śrī yī dharmmavanū

inscription.head:
No. 238.
(A.R. No. 240 of 1931-32.)
MĒḶAḌUPU, (hamlet of Ārūr), UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the field of Māle Gōvindayya.

The badly damaged record refers itself to the reign of Kikkāyi
Tāyi and her son Kulaśēkharadēva and registers gifts of lands which
were exempted from certain taxes like vosage and samudāya to
Kōṭisara-adhikāri by Vīra[nātha]dēvarasa.

The record is dated Śaka 1265, Tāraṇa Paushya śu. 1, Dhanus
9, Monday corresponding to 1344 A.D., December 6. The tithi Dhanus
9 is a mistake for Dhanus 10 and the Śaka year is wrong for 1266.

l.ka: svasti[namastuṁga śira]stuṁbi caṁdracāmara cāravē [|*] trailōkya nagarā-

l.note: The rest of the

inscription.head:
No. 239.
(A.R. No. 591 of 1929-30.)
KUMRUGŌḌU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of the Śankaranārāyaṇa temple.

The record is dated Śaka 1267, Pārthiva, Āśvayuja śu. 10,
Tulā[7], Thursday corresponding to 1345 A.D., October 6.

It registers a gift of land in Brahmapura and Kuṁmarakōḍa as
sarvamānya to Nāraṇa-nāyaka and others by the king in the presence
of the ministers while he was holding assembly in his palace
(hiryaramane) at Bārakūru.

l.ka: śrī namastuṁga śakavaruṣa 1267 neya pārtthi[va saṁvatsa]-

inscription.head:
No. 240.
(A.R. No. 603 of 1929-30.)
VĀRAMBAḶḶI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a private house.

This damage record is dated Śaka 1267, Pārthiva, Phālguṇa ba.
6, Mīna 19, Tuesday corresponding to 1346 A.D., March 14.

It registers a grant of land to Aṇṇa-Oraṁbaḷi of Brahmavūru by
the king while he was holding assembly in his palace (hiriy-aramane),
at Bārakūru in the presence of his ministers and others, for
maintaining a maṭha (?)

inscription.head:
No. 241.
(A.R. No. 360 of 1930-31.)
KANYĀNA, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field called the Mūrumaḍi-gadde.

This damaged record, in characters of about the 14th century is
dated the cyclic year Sarvadhāri, Mithuna • • • • , Friday other details
being lost. The cyclic year corresponds to 1348-49 A.D., when and the
tithi falls sometime in May-June 1348 A.D. The details cannot be
verified.

It seems to register a gift of land to Aṇṇapa Hēbāra of Hiriura
by Kikkāyi-Tāyi and Kumāradēva-Bānappa.

inscription.head:
No. 242.
(A.R. No. 526 of 1928-29.)
VARĀṄGA, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the front verandah of the Nēmīśvara-basti.

The earlier part of this undated record, in characters of about
the 14th century, contains an eulogy of Jaina preceptors, Maladhāridēva,
Mādhavachandra, Prabhāchandra, and Nēmichandra of Mūla-saṁgha,
Konḍakund-ānvaya, and Krānūr-gaṇa. The latter part gives the genealogy
of the Aḷuvas from Pāṇḍya-paṭṭiyoḍeya, his son Kaviyāḷu, his son
Kulaśekharāḷuva, who had three sons Nūrmaḍi-chakravarti, Vīra-bhūpāḷa
and Kuṇḍaṇa alias Paṇḍita-Pāṇḍya. It states that Jākalamādēvi, wife of
Kulaśēkharāḷuva had a tank built at Varāṅga.

The record announces a gift of land earlier made by a person
called Varāṅga, by Kuṇḍaṇa. The record is composed in Kannaḍa verse.

inscription.head:
No. 243.
(A.R. No. 506 of 1928-29.)
KŌṬE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This record, which is in Kannaḍa language engraved in Malayālam
characters, states that the king granted the taxes realised in
Śivasahabra[h]maṇavūra (Śivasa-Brahmavūra ?) to (the temple of) the god
at Kōṭa for the maintanance of a hand-lamp (kai-veḷaku). Adambhaṭṭ-
uḍeyarasa also appears to have participated in making the grant.

The only details of the date given is that Jupiter was in
Kumbha. The record is engraved in characters of about the 14th
century.

edition.h4: TEXT. The text is engraved in Malayalam characters and the language is in

inscription.head:
No. 244.
(A.R. No. 507 of 1928-29.)
KŌṬE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This incomplete record refers to the deity of Kōṭa and seems to
register some gift by the king.

inscription.head:
No. 245.
(A.R. No. 591 of 1929-30.)
HANDĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) built into the sluice in a field called ‘Kambaḷagadde’.

This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 1[27]7, Manmatha,
Dhanus 27, Thursday corresponding to 1355 A.D., December 24.

It registers a gift of land probably by the king when he was
holding assembly in his palace at Bārakūru. It refers to the village
Kuṁbaragōru. All other details are lost.

l.ka: nmatha saṁvatsara dhanurmāsa 27 gu śrī ma-

inscription.head:
No. 246.
(A.R. No. 569 of 1929-30.)
KUTTUPĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Two sides of a stone set up in the prākāra of the Māṅgōḍu-Subbarāya
temple.

This damaged record in characters of about the 12th century, is
dated the cyclic year Bhāva(?), Kanyā, other details being lost.

It seems to refer to social compact entered into by certain
persons of Kuttipāḍi in the presence of mahāpradhāna Narasiṅga-
heggaḍe, Voḍamadēva-parapaḷḷi and others refers to the villages of
Maṁgoḍu, Kappūru and Niḍumbūru. The name of the king is lost.
Kēśava-sēnabōva wrote the record.

inscription.head:
No. 247.
(A.R. No. 600 of 1929-30.)
HANDĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the meadow called ‘Kundāṇi-Belūr-Jeḍḍu’.

This record in charcters of about the 14th century, seems to
refer to a grant to the god (?) Paṭṇa-voḍeya, by Āḷpēndra-dēva (name
not given), his ministers and the Thousand of the village.

l.note: The rest of the record is

inscription.head:
No. 248.
(A.R. No. 239 of 1931-32.)
ĀTRĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing in the compound of Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This damaged record seems to refer to an agreement the
enjoyment of income from certain taxes, entered into by
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Oḍamadēvarasa, atikāri Nāraṇ-āḷuya, the Three-
hundred of H2ākaḷa, Twelve mahajanas of Harika and others. The
record is undated.

l.note: About seven more

inscription.head:
No. 249.
(A.R. No. 299 of 1931-32.)
BANNĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone standing in Huraḷimakki field.

This badly damaged record refers to mahāpradhāna Achyuta-
sēnabōva and to the village Banahāḍi and mentions the royal palace at
Bārahakanyāpura.

It is dated Śaka 1112, Sādhāraṇa, Vṛiśchika 12, Thursday
corresponding to 1190 A.D., November 8.

l.note:
The inscription
besides mentioning the other details, adds that ṣōmēśvara and ṁallidēva were the
sons of Vimalāditya and that the inscription was engraved during the reign of
Kulōttuṅga.

inscription.head:
No. 250.
(A.R. No. 9 of 1939-40.)
DONGALASĀNI, (HAMLET OF KURUGUṆṬPALLE) SIDDHAVATAM TALUK,
CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up by the side of the Añjanēya temple.

The inscription records that Nallachiddarasa (Nalla-siddharasa)
ruling at Nellurupaṭṭaṇa, having started out on a campaign of victory,
his subordinates Mahāpradhana, Sandhivigrahi Rēvaṇa-heggade and his
brother Pōtaṇa-heggede encountered Mallidēva and Sōmēśvaradēva, the
chiefs of Pottapidēśa at Dongalasāni; Somēśvaradēva along with twelve
other chiefs and a thousand men and dispersing several others himself
fell in the encounter. His brother Pōtaṇa along with others, reported
the success of the fight to his over lord Nallasiddha, installed perpetual
lamps at the mahāsthānas of Mallikārjuna, Tripurāntaka, Siddhavaṭa,
Aṇampura and Māhēśvara for the merit of his brother and himself.
Rēvaṇa's son Kāvaṇa, the record continues, consecrated an image of
Vishṇu at the frontier of Peḍanāḍu for the merit of his father and
his mother Dāramambikā. The inscription is followed by the Telugu and Tamil versions of which the latter,
besides mentioning the other details, adds that Sōmēśvara and Mallidēva were the
sons of Vimalāditya and that the inscription was engraved during the reign of
Kulōttuṅga.

The record is dated Śaka 1081, Pramādi, Māgha śu. 15,
arddhōdaya which details correspond to 1160 A.D., January 25.

l.ka: pratijña rūḍharāgi 1081 neya pramādi saṁvatsarada mā-
ḍaṇa huṇiseya banada yeraḍu toṟiya saṁgama • • •
yalli vīrāvatāranu saṁdhivigrahi rēvaṇa heggaḍe ku-
dureya nēṟi harisi mallidēvana nōḍisi sōmēśvara dēvanū-
raṁ virathaṁmāḍi tāvalli vīralakṣmiyaṁ kaikoṁḍu [vaiku]ṁṭha prā-
dīpa mēlu paṭṭaṁgaḷaṁ māḍisidanu ·ā rēvaṇa heggaḍe-

note.p:
At the top of the slab depicted two warriors on caparisoned horses with their
names within below the figures, the one on the left as sandhivigrahi ṟēvaṇa
heggaḍe and the other on the sight, sandhivigrahi Potana heggaḍe.

The inscription is followed by its version in Telugu characters and this in turn
by its own copy in Nagari characters which again is followed by its Tamil version
written in Tamil characters.

inscription.head:
No. 251.
(A.R. No. 530 of 1928-29.)
KORAGA, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a rice field near the village.
inscription.h4: Vīra Bhairava 1408 A.D. and Pāṇḍya Bhūpala.
inscription.p:
The record registers a gift of land in the village, Marṇe for the
worship and offering to god Pārśvanātha and for feeding the ṛishis, by
the chief Vīra-Bhairava and his son Pāṇḍya-bhūpāla who were ruling
from Keravase. The basti was got constructed by the chief in Chōḷiya-
kēri at rājadhāni Bārakūru. The gift was made at the instance of
Vasantakīrtirāu7ḷa.

It is dated Śaka 1331, Sarvadhāri, Pushya śu. 10, Thursday.
The tithi corresponds to 1408 A.D., December 27, Thursday, f.d.t. ·22.

inscription.head:
No. 252.
(A.R. No. 529 of 1928-29.)
VARĀṄGA, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab (No. 2) set up in the northern verandah of the outer prākāra of
the Nēmīśvara-basti.

This record, both in Sanskrit and Kannaḍa verse and prose, gives
a genealogical account of the Pombuchchha family from Jinadatta and
registers a gift of a village (name not clear) which was renamed
Bhairava-pura, excluding the lands originally granted to god Mahādēva
of Keravase, for the worship of and offerings to god Ādināthasvāmi in
the Nēmitīrthēśvara Chaityālaya of Varāṅga, by the chief
mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Immaḍi Bhairavarasa, son of Bommaladēvī, who was
ruling from Keravase. The gift was made over to Dēvēndrakīrti-
bhaṭṭāraka, the religious teacher of Kundakundānvaya, Balātkṛiti-gaṇa
and Śaradā-gachchha, whose genealogical account also is given. The
achievements of this teacher is recounted.

It is dated Śaka 1444, Chitrabhānu, Chitra ba. 12, Monday.
The details correspond to 1522 A.D., Mach 12 when the month was
intercalary (adhika Chaitra).

inscription.head:
No. 253.
(A.R. No. 529 of 1929-30.)
BAḌAGA KAJAKĀRU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of the Mahādēva temple.

This record is dated the cyclic year [Dundubhi], Chaitra ba. 5,
Wednesday. the details correspond to 1562 A.D., March 25 when
Doḍḍa Saṅkaṇṇa was ruling.

It seems to refers to a stipulation by the chief of Keḷadi
regarding the tax to be paid to him by the residents of the village. As
the record is badly damaged, all details are lost. It refers to Baṅgaru
and the karanikas of Maṅgalūru.

l.ka: duṁdubhi saṁvatsara cayitra ba 5 budhavāradalū śrīma[tu ||]
keḷadiya sadāśivarāya nāyakaru ku • • māgaṇi • • • grā-
tegedu koṁḍe·u • • • • • nīu maṁgalūra karaṇikara vi • na pramāṇadale

inscription.head:
No. 254.
(A.R. No. 60 of 1942-43.)
ŚRĪŚAILAM, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Bell hung in a niche in the ghanṭā-maṭham.

The record states that the bell was the gift by Piriyarājayyadēva,
king of Nañjarāyapaṭṭaṇa, son of Śrīkaṇṭharājayya and Vallabhājamma,
for their merit and that of Vīrarājayya and all others of the
Chaṅgāḷva family. The king is stated to have belonged to Sōma-vaṁśa,
Ṛik-śākha and Hārita-gōtra.

It is dated Śaka 1512, Vikṛiti, Māgha śu. 15, the tithi
corresponding to 1591 A.D., January 29. The details are not verifiable.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [|*] śālivāhana śaka 1512 suda | vikrutu saṁvatsarada
māgha śu 3 llū | śrīmanmahā dēvadēvō-

ya śāsanada vivara | jaṁgamara prasādaliṁgakke salu·udu | naṁmataṁde
śrī kaṁṭharājayyagaḷu | vallabhajaṁma-

navarigu | piriyarājayyanavarigo | vīrarājayya navarigū | kailāsavāgali
yeṁdu | caṁgāḷa vaṁśadavare |

llākailāsakke ·ayididareṁdu | pirirājayyanavaru koṭṭa gaṁṭheya
sthiraśāsana || jaṁgamada prasāda liṁgake salu·udu | śrī kaṁṭharāja-
yyagu | vallabharājaṁmagū | piriya rājayyagu jalmamukti vaḍeyitu
l.note:
ṭo the right of the inscription are engraved labelled figures of
and Vallabhājamma respectively and below them in a horiśontal that of
Piriyarāja.

inscription.head:
No. 255.
(A.R. No. 108 of 1935-36.)
VIṆṆAPAḶḶI, GOBICHETTIPALAIYAM TALUK, COIMBATORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a dolmen.

The record registers a royal gift of the village Viṇṇapaḷḷi
renamed Ñañjanagūḍa-Dēvarāyapura, situated in Ukkarada-hōbaḷi of
Uḍuvaṁka-nāḍu in Satyamaṅgala along with the hamlets (upa-grāma)
attached to it and other hamlets attached to two other villages situated
in Karavaḷi and Mēlumukha hobaḷis, to several brāhmaṇas of different
gōtras, sūtras and śākhas. For this purpose the gift land was divided
into 60 vrittis. The king described as the son of Dēvarāja-oḍeya and
grandson of Chāmarāja-oḍeya of Atrēya-gōtra, Āślāyana-sūtra and Ṛik-
śākha, is said to have purchased this area. The gift was made for the
merit of the king's ancestors in the presence of god Raṅganāthasvāmi
of Śrīraṅga-paṭṭaṇa, described as Gautama-kshētra.

It is dated Śaka 15[2]3, Virōdhikṛit, Bhādrapada śu. 15,
Monday, lunar eclipse. The details are irregular. In Śaka 1523 (1601
A.D.) the tithi occurred on September 2 Wednesday. In the cyclic year
Virōdhikṛit (Śaka 1533=1611 A.D.), the tithi occurred on September 12,
Thursday. On neither of these days did the eclipse occur.

ka.b:
saṁdu vartamānavāda virōdhikṛtsaṁvatsarada bhādrapada śu 15 lu
sō-

tra rukyākeya maisūra cāmarāja ·oḍeyara pautra rā-

inscription.head:
No. 256.
(A.R. No. 512 of 1929-30.)
ŚAṄKAGIRI, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock behind the Chennakēśava temple on the hill.

The record refers to the setting up of the images of the
dvārapālakas in the temple of Chennarāya-perumāḷ at Rāyadurga also
called Kannatūru-durga, by Muddayya of Aṅganūr who was the agent
of Daḷavāyi Nañjanāthayya who was himself the agent of the king.
The fort appears to have been under the jurisdiction of Muddaḷagādri-
nāyaka.

It is dated Śaka 1600, Piṅgaḷa, Māgha ba. 7, corresponding to
1678 A.D., February 3, the Śaka year being current. The details
cannot, however, be verified.

l.ka: yalu śrī manmahārājādhirāja rāja paramēśvara phrauḍha pra-

tāpanapramatimarāja śrī vīra cikkadēva mahārāja ·uḍeyarayyanavara
kīrtti

narru madhure-

nāyakara ma • •

yirruva kāraṇa

inscription.head:
No. 257.
(A.R. No. 155 of 1933-34.)
AMMĀPĀḶAIYAM, SALEM TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a street.

This record, which is said to be a copy of a copper-plate
inscription, registers a gift of some lands at Ammāpāḷya and Nāramaṅgala,
the cost of which was probably paid by Haidaralli into the treasury, to
Akāvallāsāhēb for maintaining the daragah and the fakīrs.

The record is dated Śaka 1681, Pramādi, Āshāḍha śu. 8, the
details being insufficient for verification. The tithi corresponds to 1759
A.D., July 2 when the week-day was Monday.

l.ka: rada ·āṣāḍa śu 8 lu śrī madrājādhirāja rājapara-
dare nīvu māḍuva phakīrara dharmmakke ·avagū • varu • •
[agi] varigrāma ga 262 || nāmanāyakana paṭa ga 207 || ḥ

l.note: The rest of the record

inscription.head:
No. 258.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1936-37.)
MĀḌUGALA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 2) set up in the compound of Kālēśvara temple.
The purport of this label record is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 259.
(A.R. No. 342 of 1936-37.)
MĀḌUGALA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 4) set up in the compound of Kālēśvara temple.
This label record reads Vikarasaṁghāta.

inscription.head:
No. 260.
(A.R. No. 345 of 1936-37.)
MĀḌUGALA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 6) set up in the compound of Kālēśvara temple.
This label record reads • • siṁgadhama[na].

group.h3:
No. 261.
(A.R. No. 330-343 of 1937-38.)
SĀTĀNIKĒṬA, ÑANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
group.p: Boulder in a field near the village.

These are short label records mentioning certain names. Of these
No. (333) refers to Aṭṭunan, probably a teacher of Māhēśvara
Kāḷāmukha sect who bore titles Utpattipiḍugu and Lōkaśilābhimāna, No.
(334) refers to Śrī-Dēvi-Ovajan described as the foot dust of stone-
cutters.

l.note:
The label is preceded
line are traces.
Some space is left here.

inscription.head:
No. 275.
(A.R. No. 505 of 1928-29.)
KŌṬE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of the Mahāliṅgeśvara temple.

The record, in characters of about the 7th century registers the
death of Raṇamaṇḍe-Aṅgupesāra-Polegan, a servant of Juddhamalla, in
a fight with Raṇakēsari who was entering Udayāpura, in the battle
agains Dharegīsa (the king ?).

inscription.head:
No. 276.
(A.R. No. 344 of 1936-37.)
MAḌGULA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 6) set up in the compound of Kālēśvara temple.

The damaged record refers to Chintāmaṇi. The purport is not
clear.

inscription.head:
No. 277.
(A.R. No. 346 of 1936-37.)
MAḌGULA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Front wall of the small Śiva shrine in the court yard of the Kālēśvara
temple.
The label reads Śrī-Anādikarttar.

inscription.head:
No. 278.
(A.R. No. 347 of 1936-37.)
MAḌUGULA, PALNAD TALUK, GUNTUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the nandi-maṇḍapa in the Kālēśvara temple.
It seems to state that this was the lamp of Saṁsārabhītadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 279.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1940-41.)
PEDDAPASUPULA, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in the backyard (doḍḍi) of the house of Juvvimānu-Pōchi-reḍḍi.

This damaged record refers to an attack on Nandakuru and
mentions Bhūpaditya who is said to have been governing Rēnāḍu. The
name of the king is lost. It records a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 280.
(A.R. No. 454 of 1940-41.)
KONAKOṆḌLA, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Boulder on the hillock called the Rasāsiddhula-guṭṭa, to the north of
the village.
This label reads Sabbanaṇdi-vanditan.

inscription.head:
No. 281.
(A.R. No. 199 of 1931-32.)
VANAMALADINNE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab (No. 5) with a panel of figures in relief, lying in a field to the
north of the village.

This record, which appears to be fragmentary, refers to a gift,
free from taxes (? sarva-parihāra) and to the village Kundanūr. It is
in characters of about the 8th-9th century.

inscription.head:
No. 282.
(A.R. No. 306 of 1935-36.)
POṬṬIPĀḌU, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab kept in the chāvadi by the side of the Narasiṁhasvāmin temple.

This damaged record refers to Dhavaḷeyarasa (of the Bāṇa
family ?) as ruling some area and mentions Ponmanapāḍi. It seems to
registers a gift to Punya-bhaṭṭa, Chandrayta-gāmuṇḍa and Pālavaṟṟa
Komareya were witnesses.

It is dated Śaka 807, [Śōbhakṟit], Āshāḍha [ba] 13, Dakshiṇāyana-
[saṁkrānti]. The details are irregular. The cyclic year corresponded to
Śaka 805 (883 A.D.) when the saṅkrānti occurred on Junee 24,
Monday, the tithi being Āshāḍha-pūrṇimā. In Śaka 807 (885 A.D.) the
saṅkrānti occurred on June 24, Thursday the tithi being Ashāḍha śu. 9.
The former might be the intended date.

l.ka:
[yanasaṁkrā]ntiyandu svasti sakaḷa jagatra[yā*]bhivandita surāsurādhīśa
paramēśvara pratīhārī-

[kṛta ba]li kulōdbhava śrīmat dhavaḷeyarasararasugeyyuttu ponmana
pāḍiya

• nūru • • • • •

rura candrayta gāmuṇḍanuṁ pālavaṟra komareyanuṁ naḍeduguḍḍeyikki
dōrini ba • • •
yasya yasya yadābhūmi tasya tasya [tadā] phalaṁ ||

inscription.head:
No. 283.
(A.R. No. 557 of 1929-30.)
HATYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH CANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying in the prākāra of the Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

This damaged epigraph, seems to record the remission of half of
the taxes due to the nakara of Purigeṟe from the merchant body
(seṭṭi-paḷḷi ?). It refers to the rule of Puḷinnarasa.

inscription.head:
No. 284.
(A.R. No. 351 of 1930-31.)
BANTRA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH CANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This record, in characters of about the 9th century A.D., seems
to record a political agreement entered into among four persons, viz.,
Narasiṅgan Durgarāja, son of Balle-oḍeya, Rāchamallan Durgarāja,
brother of Viḷarittaḷiyarasa, the Kataṁba ruler whose name is not
given and the king N®pamallarāja himself, to the effect that they
should discontinue mutual enmity and fights.

inscription.head:
No. 285.
(A.R. No. 183 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab forming part of the roof of a dolmen near the Chauḍēśvarī
temple in a field to the west of the village.

This record, in characters of about the 9th century, refers to an
attack upon Māinda-Noḻa[mba] by (Bāṇarasa) when Punniyakumārarasa
was governing Maṅgalabaḻi-12 division. This latter chief bears the
epithets Baimdhu-mahārājādhirāja, Paramēśvara and Pulvari-nātha. The
details of the record are, however, lost.

l.note: The continuation

inscription.head:
No. 286.
(A.R. No. 184 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab (No. 2) forming part of the roof of a dolmen near the
Chauḍēśvarī temple in a field to the west of the village.

This fragmentary record refers to a gift of land situated below a
tank. All other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 287.
(A.R. No. 185 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in the sluice of the tank to the north of the village.

The record registers a gift of 15 guḷa of land as ēripaṭṭi,
probably for the upkeep of the sluice caused to be built by
Mulagundura Ballamma-seṭṭiti.

inscription.head:
No. 288.
(A.R. No. 188 of 1931-32.)
KURAPPALLE (HAMLET OF ĀRAḌIGUṆṬA), PUNGANUR TALUK,
CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two pieces of a broken slab near the Āñjanēya temple.

This broken record registers a gift of land as kalnāḍu, by the
king and others in memory of a hero by name Niḍumban, who died
in a cattle-raid.

l.note: ṁore than one line appear

inscription.head:
No. 289.
(A.R. No. 162 of 1933-34.)
KĀPPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Three stray stones lying in the precincts of the Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

The first two fragments seem to refer to a gift. The third, in
Tamil, to the maṇḍalika of Pudu-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 290.
(A.R. No. 294 of 1935-36.)
MADDIKERA, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Fragment of a slab set up near the Bijjappa-Nāyaḍu's hall.

It seems to register some gift of land and garden and refers to
puṇṇame. Details are all lost.

inscription.head:
No. 291-292.
(A.R. Nos. 455-56 of 1940-41.)
KONAKOṆḌLA, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Boulders on the hillock called the village.

These two label records read (1) Śrī [Sūlā]komaran and (2) Śrī
[Uri]gapasiṇḍi, respectively.

inscription.head:
No. 293.
(A.R. No. 180 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 9) set up in a field to the west of the village.

The record is dated Śaka 886 corresponding to 964-65 A.D. It
registers a gift of land by the king and the ūru in memory of a hero
(name lost) who died during a cattle-raid at Maṅgala. It states that
Vajjaḻadēva was ruling over Puli-nāḍu.

l.note: This and the subsequent lines are

inscription.head:
No. 294.
(A.R. No. 324 of 1937-38.)
PRĀTAKŌṬA, NANDIKOTAKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of the Nāgēśvarasvāmin temple.

This record states that Mādhayya had a temple with a gōpura
and tōraṇa constructed for god Mādhavēśvara-bhaṭāra.

It is dated the cyclic year Prajōtpatti, śu. 10, Monday, Chittā-
nakshatra. On palaeographical grounds the record may be assigned to
the 10th century in which case these details regularly correspond to 971
A.D., June 29, Monday, f.d.t. ·47 and f.d.n. ·04. The month would be
Āshāḍha. It gives further details of date, viz., Śiva-yōga, Kauḷa-karaṇa
and Siṁga-lagna, Bṛihaspati in Udaya.

inscription.head:
No. 295.
(A.R. No. 585 of 1929-30.)
PADU-ALEVUR, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of Durgā-Paramēśvarī temple.

This record seems to register a gift of land for a perpetual lamp
in the temple of a goddess (name not clear), by Obaliabbe of
Kokkaraṇi, in the kingdom of Sāntaradēva.

It is dated the cyclic year Virōdhikṛit, Dhanus, Jupiter in
Kuṁbha.

l.note: The continuation is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 296.
(A.R. No. 175 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 4) set up in a field to the west of the village.

This badly damaged record seems to refer to the capture of 300
bulls by a certain [kānpu] of the Baḷañjigas of Maṅgala.

inscription.head:
No. 297.
(A.R. No. 176 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 5) set up in a field to the west of the village.

This record registers the death of Bīra-Baḷoja, when Bīra
Baḷanjinga Vemma[ra*]sa son of Siṅghapuli captured 300 bulls. It states
that Vajjaradēva was ruling the country during the period.

inscription.head:
No. 298.
(A.R. No. 178 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 7) set up in a field to the west of the village.

This record registers a gift of land under the tank called the
Baḍaṅgāni-keṟe, by the king and the ūru of Maṅgala in memory of a
hero whose name is not clear, who died during a cattle-raid. It states
that Vajradēva was ruling over Puli-nāḍu.

inscription.head:
No. 299.
(A.R. No. 179 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 8) set up in a field to the west of the village.

This badly damaged record seems to register the death of an
individual during a cattle-raid. Māchemarāja set up the stone.

inscription.head:
No. 300.
(A.R. No. 181 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 10) set up in a field to the west of the village.

This registers the death of Sattiyapaḷi (?) grandson of Chaṭṭa-
gamuṇḍa in a skirmish when Pallavaraya, bearing the epithet gaṇḍaroḷ-
gaṇḍa, captured the cattle of Maṅgala.

l.ka: ṟu goṇḍu pa[riye] caṭṭu gamuṇḍana mammaga

inscription.head:
No. 301.
(A.R. No. 182 of 1931-32.)
BŌḌINĀYANIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 11) set up in a field to the west of the village.

This damaged record refers to Vajjaradēva who was governing
Puli-nāḍu and registers the death of an individual during a cattle-raid.
The details are, however, lost.

inscription.head:
No. 302.
(A.R. No. 191 of 1931-32.)
DĒSIDOḌḌI (NEAR VANAMALADINNE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field in front of the deserted Vishṇu temple.

This broken record seems to refers to the construction of a
sluice of the tank (called Paneguṇṭe ?) for the merit of Jannabaliga,
son of Pṛithivirāma-seṭṭi. It mentions Sāmuseṭṭi.

l.note: The continuation is

inscription.head:
No. 303.
(A.R. No. 193 of 1931-32.)
DĒSIDOḌḌI (NEAR VANAMALADINNE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Side-slab of a dolmen (No. 2) in front of the deserted Vishṇu temple.

This worn out record registers the death of Puḷiyamma, the
younger brother of Pṛithivirāma-seṭṭi, son of Balimurka-gavuṇḍa of
Eḍeyūr, Dōramma and others in a battle. It mentions a Noḻaṁba and
refers to Mārajavāḍi and to a gift of land.

inscription.head:
No. 304.
(A.R. No. 166 of 1933-34.)
KĀPPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Hero-stone (No. 3) set up in a field about two furlongs to east of the
Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

It registers the death of Kāla Vaḷpuḷa Bikkiyaṇṇa and his son
Beḍecha (?) along with Māsarayya, (probably their master).

inscription.head:
No. 305.
(A.R. No. 167 of 1933-34.)
KĀPPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 4) lying in the same place.

This record states that the image (of the hero) is that of
Mārammarasa who died along with Māsarasa (probably his master). Cf.
No. 318 above.

inscription.head:
No. 306.
(A.R. No. 169 of 1933-34.)
KĀPPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 6) set up in the same place.
It registers the death of Janaga [Pōta]rasa along with his uncle.

inscription.head:
No. 307.
(A.R. No. 171 of 1933-34.)
KĀPPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone (No. 8) in the same place.

This fragmentary record refers to three individuals Gaṁga,
Gaṁgaṁ-Raṇaṁga and Malla and to a certain Mōḍamma who was
ruling the nāḍu.

l.note: The continuation is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 308.
(A.R. No. 83 of 1944-45.)
SAṆḌŪR, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pedestal in an enclosure near the Pātālēśvara shrine in the
Kumārasvāmin temple on the Kumārasvāmi hill.

This is dated Śaka 9[6]7, Pārthiva Vaiśākha śu. 6, Thursday
corresponding 1042 A.D., April 25.

It records the installation of (an image of) a peacock by the
goldsmith (akkasāle) Chandāchāri, son off Hampāchāri of Orvvay. It
refers to Bayna-kula.

inscription.head:
No. 309.
(A.R. No. 318 of 1937-38.)
KURUKUNDA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying behind the Śiva temple.

This badly damaged record contains two dates 1) Śaka 980,
Vi[ḷaṁbi], Vaisakha-puṇṇame corresponding to 1058 A.D., April 11,
the details being insufficient for verification. 2) Śaka [981], Vaiśākha-
Puṇṇame, Thursday, corresponding to 1059 A.D., April 29, Thursday,
f.d.t. ·43.

The extant portion refers to a Telugu-Chōḷa chief. All other
details are lost.

l.note: The

inscription.head:
No. 310.
(A.R. No. 319 of 1937-38.)
KURUKUNDA, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Sculptured slab buried near the platform in the centre of the village.

This fragmentary record refers to a mānya of land. All other
details are lost.

l.note: The slab is broken on all

inscription.head:
No. 311.
(A.R. No. 451 of 1940-41.)
KONAKOṆḌLA, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Boulder on the hillock called the Rasāsiddhula-guṭṭa to the north of
the village.
It mentions the nisidi of Nāgasēnadēva.

inscription.head:
No. 312.
(A.R. No. 531 of 1929-30.)
UḶIPĀḌI, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Sides of the balipītha in front of the Paḍu temple.

This incomplete and damaged record is dated Śaka 1041, Viḷaṁbi
Makara-saṅkramaṇa, Utare-nakshatra, Wednesday, corresponding to
1118, A.D., December 25, f.d.n., ·66. The pūrnimā referred to in the
record occurred on Sunday the 29th.
It refers to the rule of some king and mentions Basava.

l.ka:
[svasti saka] nṛpakālātīta saṁvatsaraṁgaḷ 1041 neya biḷaṁbisaṁvatsarada
makara vyāḷa • • • • • • • • • • budhavāra ·utare nakṣatraṁ makarā-

inscription.head:
No. 313.
(A.R. No. 468 of 1928-29.)
SŌMĒŚVARA, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Sculptured slab set up near the kitchen in the prākāra of
Sōmanāthēśvara temple.

This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 1117 corresponding to
1195-96 A.D., all other details being lost. The purport of the record is
not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 314.
(A.R. No. 343 of 1930-31.)
KĪḶPĀḌI, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field.

This record registers a gift of land to a certain Vaidya (name
not clear) by Dēvarasa of Komāramaṅgala and others.

It is dated the cyclic year Siddhārthi, Vṛishabha 15, [Saturday],
probably corresponding to 1199 A.D., May 9 Sunday, which might
be the date intended.

inscription.head:
No. 315.
(A.R. No. 520 of 1928-29.)
NELLIKARU, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar of the maṇḍapa of the Anantanātha basti.

This record in Sanskrit verse states that the mandira of the
chaitya was caused to be built by Mañjeṇa alias Koṁṇa-bhūpati.

inscription.head:
No. 316.
(A.R. No. 521 of 1928-29.)
NELLIKARA, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pillar built into the wall at the left of entrance into the maṇḍapa of
the Anantanātha basti.

This record states that the pillar (?) was caused to be made by
Dēvachandradhara, with the property of Kalyāṇakīrti-dēva, disciple of
Lalitakīrti-bhaṭṭāraka.

inscription.head:
No. 317.
(A.R. No. 345 of 1930-31.)
PUTTŪRU, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Mahāliṅgēśvarasvāmin temple.

This damaged record mentions about the construction of a tank
and prohibition of fishing therein. It refers to the temple of Huttūru
and to Pāṇḍyarasa. The extant portion of the date mentions the
cyclic year Sādhāraṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 318.
(A.R. No. 287 of 1935-36.)
KUṄKANŪRU, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KARNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Sculptured slab set up in the Ramaliṅgēśvara temple east side.
It refers to a [Sō]ma-gāvuḍa as a devotee of god Rāmēśvaradēva.

inscription.head:
No. 319.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1941-42.)
RĀCHĀNAPALLE, ANANTAPUR TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up by the road-side near the village.

This register an agreement (manevārtte) regarding rents etc.,
between mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Chandarasa and Nāgaya-gavuṇḍa described
as his pādamūli. It refers to Koḍiyame-40 division and mentions
mahāpradhāna, mane-verggaḍe, kari-tureya-paṭṭasāhaṇi Āneya Kēsimayya.
It also refers to an attack made on the village.

It is dated the 17th year Rudhirōdgāri, Vaiśākha ba. 5,
Thursday. On palaeographical grounds the record may be assigned to
the 12th-13th century.

inscription.head:
No. 320.
(A.R. No. 226 of 1937-38.)
ELLĀREḌḌIPALLE, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in Survey No. 475 near the canal about three furlongs to
the south-west of the village.

This badly damaged record refers to mahāmaṇḍalēśvara
Sōmēśvaradēva-mahārāja bearing the epithet Trailōkyamalla-Bhujabala-
Vīranārāyaṇa as governing Muḷiki-300, Honnahaḷi-90 and Peṇḍekal-800
divisions from [Vallū]rapaṭṭaṇa and registers a gift made over to
Gōkarṇarāśi-gurudēva disciple (devotee ?) of Kamaḷaśaṅkaradēva. All
other details are lost.

The extant portion of the record refers to the date as Śaka
1128, Prabhava. The Śaka year is wrong for 1129 corresponding to
1207-08 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 321.
(A.R. No. 530 of 1929-30.)
MUNDKŪRU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the outer prākāra of Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This damaged record, in characters of about the 13th century, is
dated the cyclic year Vijaya, Mēsha 26, Sunday, probably corresponding
to 1293 A.D., April 19.

It refers toa gift, details of which are lost, probably made over
to the sthānapati of the Lōkēśvara temple. The tenants and the
nāḍavaru are stated to have set up bammottiyakallu which may be the
same as the stone on which this record is inscribed. It refers to a
king, probably a local chief of the Sōma-kula.

inscription.head:
No. 322.
(A.R. No. 354 of 1930-31.)
HEMMĀḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up by the side of the central shrine in
Lakshmīnārāyaṇadēva temple.

This badly damaged record registers a gift, for burning a
perpetual lamp and supplying flower garlands in the temple of
Lakshmīnārāyaṇadēva. It refers to Bārakūru. All other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 323.
(A.R. No. 354 of 1930-31.)
HEMMADI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up by the side of the central shrine in
Lakshmīnārāyaṇadēva temple.

This badly damaged record registers a gift, for burning a
perpetual lamp and supplying flower garlands in the temple of
Lakshmīnārāyaṇadēva. It refers to Bārakūru. All other details are lost.

l.note: The rest

inscription.head:
No. 324.
(A.R. No. 373 of 1930-31.)
KUNJURU, (NEAR YELLŪRU), UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the Durgāparamēśvari temple.
This record is very badly damaged and all the details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 325.
(A.R. No. 243 of 1931-32.)
HĀNĒHAḶḶI, (SAME AS MŪḌAKĒRI), UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Back of a wall set up outside the west wall of the Sōmanatha temple.

This badly damaged record refers to some gift of land and
other money-incomes by some seṭṭis. It is dated the cyclic year
Pārthiva, in the 13th day of the summer season, Saṁkrānti, the details
being insufficient for verification.

inscription.head:
No. 326.
(A.R. No. 302 of 1931-32.)
ŚIRIYĀRA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing near Girike-maṭha.
This record is too badly damaged to make out any details.

inscription.head:
No. 327.
(A.R. No. 353 of 1938-39.)
CHINTALAPAṬṬŪRU, CUDDAPAH TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Sculptured slab lying in front of the Pushpēśvara temple on the hill.

This record seems to register the death of a hero. It refers to
Siddavaṭṭa as the southern door-entrance to Śriparvata. All the other
details are lost.
2. The rest of the record is badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 328.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1943-44.)
RĀMGAḌH, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero stone set up near the Rāmadēva temple.

It registers the death of Baichaya, the leader of the cavalry, in a
fight fought on behalf of his master Kaṁpilarāya, against Ballāḷarāya
during the latter's expedition.

It is dated the cyclic year [Dundubhi], Phāḷguṇa śu. 15,
Monday. The record is in characters of about the 14th century.
Hence the details may probably correspond to 1323 A.D., February 21.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 329.
(A.R. No. 258 of 1931-32.)
KACHCHŪRU (KŌṬAKĒRI), UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Broken slab leaning against the south wall near the hōmakunḍa in the
Pañchaliṅgēśvara temple.

This damaged and broken record refers to a gift probably to god
Mārkaṇḍēśvara-dēva. The details are all, however, lost. It refers to
mahāpradhāna Maleya-daṇṇāyaka as governing Bārakūru-rājya.

It is dated Śaka 1285, Sōbhakṛit, Kārttika śu. 8, Sunday
corresponding to 1363 A.D., October 15, Sunday, f.d.t. ·15.

l.ka: du śrīmanu mahā pradhānaṁ maleya daṁṇāya

inscription.head:
No. 330.
(A.R. No. 115 of 1935-36.)
KAṬLETALA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two sides of a slab set up in front of the well called Pustaka-tīrtha.

This badly damaged record seems to register a gift, made over to
Bīru-seṭi, son-in-law of Sōmi-seṭi and Doḍatale-seṭi. It reforms to an
atikāri and to god Kaitiladanātha (?).

l.note:
The rest of the
badly damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 331.
(A.R. No. 555 of 1929-30.)
UPPUNDA, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in afield called ‘Hāḍimane-hola’.

This damaged record in characters of about the 14th century,
seems to register a gift of land to Nāraṇa-aḍigaḷu, in the presence of
the nāḍu of Uppuguṅḍa and others, by the king Kātarasa of the
Aḍiyara-kula. It mentions Kōlarasa and Aṇṇa-sēnabōva.

It is dated the cyclic year Naḷa Māgha-amāvasya, Monday
probably corresponding to 1377 A.D., February 8, the being Sunday.

l.ka: karīṁdra ka[ṁ*]ṭhīravaṁ paranārī sahōdaraṁ husivara sū-

inscription.head:
No. 332.
(A.R. No. 531 of 1928-29.)
MIYYAR, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in a garden.

This is dated Śaka 1307, Krōdhana, Kārttika śu. 1, Sunday.
The details are irregular, the tithi corresponding to 1385 A.D., October
5 when the weekday was Thursday.

It registers a gift of land, as sarvamānya, to god Mahādēva of
Miyara by Kōṭiseṭi alias Binnāṇi and several others, when Vīra
Chennarasa-voḍeya is stated to be ruling the country.

inscription.head:
No. 333.
(A.R. No. 576 of 1929-30.)
KOḌAVŪRU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up near the entrance into Vaḍabaṇḍēśvara (Balarāma) temple.

It registers a gift (of land where the stone was originally set up?)
in Kani-bayalu to god Oḍabaṇḍa-dēva by Śaṅkara-chaṅkyara. The
record, in characters of about the 14th century, is dated the cyclic year
Raktākshi, Hangu (Paṅguṇi ?) 27, Tuesday, probably corresponding to
1335 A.D., March 22, Wednesday which might be the intended date.

inscription.head:
No. 334.
(A.R. No. 472 of 1928-29.)
BANGRAKULURU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into the ground near a well in a field.

This worn out record is dated Śaka 1311, Śukla, Mīna 3,
Sunday corresponding to 1390 A.D., February 26 when the week-day
was Saturday.
It seems to register gifts of land probably to god Mañjanāthadēva.

l.ka: ṭādaḍe śrī maṁjinātha dēvara • • • • • • • • • • yaru kāriya-

inscription.head:
No. 335.
(A.R. No. 499 of 1928-29.)
NĪLĀVARA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Right side of the slab (No. 8) set up in thee prākāra of
Mahishāsuramardini temple.

This record assignable on palaeographical grounds to the 15th
century is dated the cyclic year Vikrama, Chaitra ba. 30, Friday,
solar eclipse. The given details correspond regularly to 1400 A.D.,
March 26, when the month was Adhika Chaitra.

The inscription records the renovation of the temple of Durggādēvī
by the sixteen of the village headed by Bayiraṇṇa and registers a gift
of specified quantities of rice for the daily worship of and offerings
to the goddess for the merit of mahāpradhāna Īrugappa-daṇṇāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 336.
(A.R. No. 473 of 1928-29.)
BANGRAKUḶŪRU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field.

This completely wornout record seems to register a gift of land.
No details can, however, be made out.

l.note: Lines 1-24 are completely worn out.

inscription.head:
No. 337.
(A.R. No. 512 of 1928-29.)
MĀṆŪRU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record refers to gift of land for feeding
Brāhmaṇas. It mentions Maṇavūr. All details are lost.

l.ka: daṁ baḍagalu paḍuvalu ·a • ya kēśavana gaḍiyiṁdaṁ mūḍalu • • •

edition.p:
This is badly worn out, the only expression that
can be made out in line 4 being.

inscription.head:
No. 338.
(A.R. No. 602 of 1929-30.)
VĀRAṀBAḶḶI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in a field called ‘Tenkabirti’.

This badly damaged record refers to a gift of land by Maleya-
daṇṇāyaka after purchase from the mahājanas, and mentions Brahmāra
and two persons Aṇṇa-Vōraṁbaḷi and Vishṇu-Vōraṁbaḷi. The extant
portion of the record refers to a solar eclipse.

inscription.head:
No. 339.
(A.R. No. 362 of 1930-31.)
MARVANTE, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Mahārajasvāmin (Vishṇu) temple.

This badly damaged record registers gifts of lands. The extant
portion of the date refers to Śaka 1 • • •, all other details of date,
king, etc. being lost. It refers to a nāyaka.

l.ka:
svastiśrī gaṇā • • • • • • • • • • namastuṁga śirastuṁbi caṁdracāmara
cāravē

• • • • • • • • • • naḍava bha mū 6 • • • dakke naḍavakke mūḍe • • mahā
• • • • • • • • • •

l.note: The rest of the inscription is completely damaged.

inscription.head:
No. 340.
(A.R. No. 370 of 1930-31.)
BANTAKALLU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero stone set up in a grove by the roadside.

The record, in characters of about the 14th century, registers an
agreement between Kunda-heggaḍe and Kinnikka-heggaḍe who were
administering Yermmuḷa on one side and the Mūḍila-paksha, the nāḍu,
the baḷi and their 5000 followers on the other side. It refers to the
dishonouring of Pāṇḍyappoḍeya of Kārakaḷa and mentions Pādūru and
Uḷadūru. It is dated the cyclic year Śōbhakṛit and other details are not
given.

l.ka: kiṁnikka heggaḍeyavaru voṁdāgi yermmuḷa mēle rājakārya

dake hāridaṁtā kallu

inscription.head:
No. 341.
(A.R. No. 248 of 1931-32.)
HĀNĒHAḶḶI, (SAME AS MŪḌAKĒRI) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Broken slab lying in front of the house of Lakshmī-nārāyaṇa-śāstri
near the Sōmanātha temple.

This damaged record registers gifts of land, made after purchase,
by the people and the four sēnabōvas of Hanneyahaḷi to the good
Sōmanātha for feeding twelve brāhmaṇas in the maṭha attached to it.
It also records gifts made to the several temple servants for their
services.

inscription.head:
No. 342.
(A.R. No. 249 of 1931-32.)
HĀNĒHAḶḶI, (SAME AS MŪḌAKĒRI) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Back of a broken slab set up at the main entrance into the
Sōmanātha temple.

This fragmentary record registers a gift of land. The details are
all, however, lost.

edition.h4: TEXT. The beginning of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 343.
(A.R. No. 251 of 1931-32.)
KACHCHŪRU, (KŌṬAKĒRI) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying near the hōmakuṇḍa in the Pañchaliṅgēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record registers a gift of paddy and money to
the deities, Sōmanāthadēva of Maṇigārakeṟi, Mādhavadēva and
Pañchaliṅgadēva and to several temple servants.

inscription.head:
No. 344.
(A.R. No. 252 of 1931-32.)
KACHCHŪRU, (KŌṬAKĒRI) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Broken slab (No. 2) lying near the hōmakuṇḍa in the
Pañchaliṅgēśvara temple.

This damaged record registers a gift of land by Śrīdhara-Vaidya,
son of Lakshmīdhara-Vaidya after purchasing the same from Kōṭiyaṇṇa-
seṭṭi. The details of the grant are lost. It mentions [Ma]lleya-daṇṇāyaka
and Ba[yirappo]ḍēya.

inscription.head:
No. 345.
(A.R. No. 259 of 1931-32.)
KACHCHŪRU, (KŌṬAKĒRI) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Three broken slabs built into the steps of the tank opposite to the
Pañchaliṅgēśvara temple.

This record seems to register a gift of land to the god
Mārkaṇḍēśvaradēva in Kōṭeyakēri, probably by Nāgappadaṇṇāyaka and
the seṭṭikāṟas. The former appears to have borne the title mahāpradhāna
It refers to Śaṅkaradēva-oḍeya The name of the king and other details
are all lost.

l.ka:
• • • • • ya voḷage ·uḷa gade 5 kaṁ bittuva bede hāne 40 ṟama •
• • • • •
sahā hoḷara sāvaṁtara gade 4 kkaṁ bittuva bede hāne 40 • • • • •

inscription.head:
No. 346.
(A.R. No. 286 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀḶA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken stone lying on a raised pial near the Bhaṇḍāri-kēri-maṭha.

This fragmentary record seems to register a gift of land and
money, for offerings and burning a perpetual lamp probably in a
maṭha by Tirumakaṁbaḷi and Saṅkuseṭi. The gift was entrusted to
Raghuvara-tīrtha-śrīpāda. Karaṇika Mallarasa of Bārakūru wrote the
record.

inscription.head:
No. 347.
(A.R. No. 344 of 1931-32.)
HĀVAÑJE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing in Survey No. 3 in the hamlet of Muggēri.

This badly damaged record refers to a gift of land and mentions
Narasiṅga-heggaḍe. All details are lost.

l.note: The rest

inscription.head:
No. 348.
(A.R. No. 353 of 1934-35.)
HAMPI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pillar in a deserted temple near a tank behind the Prasanna-Āñjanēya
temple.

It states that Vīra Kaṁpiladēva, son of Mummaḍi Siṅgeyanāyaka,
a devotee of god Saṅgamēśvara had a temple constructed and installed
therein liṅgas in memory of his parents Siṅgeya-nāyaka, and Mādānāyakiti,
and Perumeyanāyaka.

inscription.head:
No. 349.
(A.R. No. 450 of 1940-41.)
KONAKOṆḌLA, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab in the well near the mosque in the bazār.

The record states that the well as also the Mayilārdēva-chatra
were constructed by Ayipadēva-ayya.

It is dated the cyclic year Āṅgira, Mārggaśira, other details not
being given.

inscription.head:
No. 350.
(A.R. No. 76 of 1941-42.)
ĀDVĀNI, ADONI TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Back of the rock-cut image of Gaṇēśa close to the entrance of the
Bāvāji-maṭha.
The record is too badly damaged and nothing can be out.

inscription.head:
No. 351.
(A.R. No. 340 of 1931-32.)
BOMMĀRUBEṬṬU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing in the Mūḍa-maṭha.

It records a gift of rice for worship, offerings, burning a
perpetual lamp and feeding brahmins on festival days in the maṭha of
god Janārdana-mūrti by Kela-heggaḍa Mañjuseṭṭi of Bammara-beṭṭu. The
said maṭha was caused to be built by Pāṇḍi-sāvanta.

It is dated the cyclic year Viśvāvasu, Dakshiṇāyana, Siṁha 15,
Ashṭami, Sunday. On palaeographical grounds the record may be
assigned to the 14th-15th century. The details do not tally in any of
the given cyclic year occuring in the period.

inscription.head:
No. 352.
(A.R. No. 341 of 1930-31.)
SĪMANTŪRU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 3) set up in the Janārdanasvāmin temple.

This record gives in detail the conditions of agreement reached
between Chennaya Mēlaṇṭa alias Kunda-heggaḍe and his nephew on
one side and Kinnika-sāmanta alias Bācha-heggaḍe and his nephew
Kānta-heggaḍe on the other, in respect of the possession of some lands
about which they had become estranged.

It is dated Śaka 1333, Vikṛita, Mīna 13, Monday corresponding
to 1411 A.D., March 9.
1) Each line commences with a circle.

inscription.head:
No. 353.
(A.R. No. 335 of 1930-31.)
INUVALLI, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two broken slabs lying in the Durgātemple.

This badly damaged record is dated Śaka [1]335, Vijaya,
[Śrāvaṇa] 1, corresponding to 1413, A.D., July 30. The details are
not verifiable.

It seems to register gifts of income to a deity and of oil for
burning a lamp in the temple in the month of Vṛiśchika. It refers to
the village Yinavaḷi. It is fragmentary and details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 354.
(A.R. No. 321 of 1931-32.)
KUDI, (No. 34) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) standing in Survey No. 97.

This is dated Śaka 1335, Nandana, [Phālguṇa] śu. 12, Sunday,
corresponding to 1413 A.D., February 12. The Śaka year was current.

It registers a gift of land for offerings to god Śaṅkaranārāyaṇadēva,
made after purchase, by Bemmaṇañcha Toḷaha who was enjoying the
kumāravṛitti under his elder brother whose name is not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 355.
(A.R. No. 319 of 1931-32.)
KUDI, (No. 34) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the temple of Chapparamaṭha Gōpālakrishṇadeva.

This record registers a gift of land by Kōṭiyaṇṇa of Hāruḷahaḷḷi,
nephew (aḷiya) of [Chammu]-Bhaṇḍārināyaka for feeding brāḥmaṇas on
certain occasions, probably in a maṭha. The gift land appears to have
been purchased by the donor. It refers to Bemmaṇa [Kuva]ri Toḷaha
who appears to have been in charge of the area, enjoying it as
kumāravṛitti.

It is dated Śaka 13[3]6, Jaya, Mēsha, other details of date not
being give. It corresponds to 1414-15 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 356.
(A.R. No. 328 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 3) set up in the inner prākāra of the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This damaged record is dated Śaka 1339, Durmukha, Śrāvaṇa.
Other details are not given. It corresponds to 1416-17 A.D., the Śaka
year being current.

It registers a gift of land made by Mādāḍi Toḷaha for feeding
Brāhmaṇas daily in the temple of Mahādēva at Surāla. The record was
caused to be engraved by Bemmaṇa-kōri Toḷaha. It mentions Neṇagunda-
30.

inscription.head:
No. 357.
(A.R. No. 330 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 5) in the inner prākāra of the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

It registers a gift of land by Kōṭi-seṭṭi of Sūrāla for feeding
three Brāhmaṇas in the temple of that place during the period of
Bemmaṇa-Kōri Toḷaha, when Hosabukōri was enjoying the kumāravṛitti.

It is dated Śaka 1339, Durmukhi, Śrāvaṇa, the details not being
sufficient for verification. It corresponds to 1416-17 A.D., the Śaka year
being current.

inscription.head:
No. 358.
(A.R. No. 476 of 1928-29.)
ADDŪRU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up near the Patel's house in the village.

This record is dated Śaka 1356, Ānanda, Mēsha Prathamā,
Sunday. The details given correspond to 1434 A.D., March 27 when
the weekday was Saturday, not Sunday.

It records a gift of land to a Jōgi-purusha called Jugādi-kuṇḍala
by Jōgi Voḍeya alias Chavuṭa.

inscription.head:
No. 359.
(A.R. No. 327 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) set up in the inner prākāra of the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This record registers a gift of land by Kuchchupāḍi, son of
Mādāḍi Toḷaha to the temple of Mahādēva for feeding a Brāhmaṇa
therein. It was made during the period of Nāgañcha Toḷaha and
Bemmaṇañcha.

It is dated Śaka 135[6], Siddhārthi, Āśvija śu. 5, Tuesday. These
details are somewhat irregular. The Saka and the cyclic years do not
tally. If, however, the cyclic year is a mistake for Ānanda the other
given details regularly correspond with 1434 A.D., September 7,
Tuesday, f.d.t. ·02. This appears to be the intended date.

inscription.head:
No. 360.
(A.R. No. 352 of 1930-31.)
VIṬLA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Copper sheet preserved in the palace.

The record states that Muṇḍi-seṭṭi-śēkhara had the golden pinnacle
(kalaśa) set up on the temple of Pañchaliṅgadēva of Ishṭakapura, on
behalf of Māṇimaida nephew (aḷiya) of Ḍoṁba-veggaḍe alias Kinyaṇṇakāva,
who was cured of disease. The donor was himself the nephew of
Evaṇa-svāmi son of Ḍoṁba-veggaḍe alias Kuṁñi-śēkhara.

It is dated Śaka 1358, Rākshasa, Mīna 17, Wednesday. The
details are irregular. The tithi corresponds to 1436 A.D., March 12
when the weekday was Monday, the Śaka year being current.

l.ka:
śrī sakavaruṣa 1358 ya rākṣasa saṁbatsarada mīnamāsa 17 bu
iṣṭakāpūrada śrī paṁcaliṁga dēvarige kuṁṅaśēkharā ḍoṁba veggaḍeyara
maga [va]vaṇa svāmiya-

ra ·aḷiya muṁḍiseṭṭi śēkharu kinyaṁṇakā[va]rāda ḍoṁbuveggaḍeyara
aḷiya maṇimaidana vyādhiparihārārtthavāgi samarppisida suvarṇa kalaśake
maṁgala mahāśrī

inscription.head:
No. 361.
(A.R. No. 367 of 1928-29.)
TIRUPPĀLAIVANAM, PONNERI TALUK, CHINGLEPUT DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pedestal of the metallic image of Naṭārāja in the Tiruppālēśvara
temple.

The record is dated Śaka 1362, expressed by the chronogram
rītiṁlōke, Raudri, Kārttika śu. 13, Friday. The tithi corresponds to
1440 A.D., November 7 when week-day was Monday.

It states that the pīṭha of the Naṭarāja image was got done by
Avasaraṁ Braṁmapa-nāyaka, son of Timmaṇa, the minister at Udayagiri.

l.ka:
svastiśrī śakavaruṣagaḷu 1362 • ra • raudri saṁvatsara kārttika śu
13 su
śubhamastu maṁgaḷa mahā śrī śrī śrī śrī śrī

inscription.head:
No. 362.
(A.R. No. 537 of 1929-30.)
BAINDŪRU, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in the courtyard of a private house near the Local
Fund Dispensary.

The is dated Śaka 1371, Śukla, Chaitra śu. 10, Thursday
corresponding to 1449 A.D., April 2, the weekday being, however,
Wednesday. This date is identical with that of No. 536/29-30 above.

It records a gift of lands to god Pārśvanātha-tīrthaṅkara of
Baidūr by Mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Indagarasa-oḍeya, son of Saṅgirāya-oḍeya,
who was governing Hāḍuvaḷi-rājya.

edition.h4: TEXT. This is engraved in continuation of No. 140 about.

inscription.head:
No. 363.
(A.R. No. 324 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of Mahāliṇgēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1370 (current), Prajōtpatti, Chaitra śu. 1,
Thursday. The details are slightly irregular. The cyclic year given
corresponded to Śaka 1373 (expired) when the tithi occurred on 1451 A.
D., March 4. In Śaka 1370, Vibhava, the tithi occured on 1448 A.D.,
March 6 when the week-day was Wednesday. The former, therefore,
appears to be the intended date.

It registers a gift of land by Bemmiseṭṭy for feeding seven
brahmiṇas in the maṭha attached to the temple of Mahādēva of Sūrāla,
during the time of Nāgañcha Toḷaha and his younger brother
Bemmaṇañcha Toḷaha.

inscription.head:
No. 364.
(A.R. No. 333 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 8) set up in the inner prākāra of Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

It registers a gift of land by Kōṭyaṇṇa-Koṭhāri for feeding a
brāhmaṇa daily in the satra attached to the Mahādēva temple of
Surāla, during the period of Nāgañcha Toḷaha and his younger brother
Bemmaṇañcha Toḷaha. The record is damaged at the end.

It is dated Śaka 1370, Prajāpati, Chaitra śu. 1, Thursday. In
the given cyclic year corresponding to Śaka 1373 (not 1370) the tithi
fell on 1451 A.D., March 4.

l.ka: bha mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē [||*] svastiśrī jayādbhudaya ṣaka

ka.b: vyavahārika prajōtpatya saṁvatsarada caiyitra śu 1 gu-

ke barada śilāśāsanada kramaveṁteṁdare śrīmahā dē-
vara saṁnidhiyali māḍida dharṁ[ma] dinake ·obba brāhmaṇa-
ṁge tsatra naḍava[da*]ke māḍida dharṁmake ·allinaḍava bāḷina
koṁḍa bāḷina catusīmeya vivara mūḍalu me-

inscription.head:
No. 365.
(A.R. No. 332 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 7) set up in the inner prākāra of Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This record is dated Śaka 1373, Prajōtapatti, Phālguṇa śu. 1,
Monday, corresponding to 1452 A.D., February 21.

It registers a gift of land by Nāgañcha Toḷaha and his younger
brother Bemmaṇañcha who was enjoying the kumāravṛitti for feeding
brāhmaṇas in the satra attached to the temple of Mahādēva at Surāla
and set up by Mādāḍi Toḷaha. It was entrusted to madhyastha
Vāsudēva-chhātra, son of Kēśava-chhātra of Tekkaṭṭi. It also records
another similar gift by Rāma-uḍupa.

The supplementary record found at the end registers another gift
of land made for a similar purpose by Nāgañcha Toḷaha and
Bemmaṇañcha to Mādhava sēnaba and Purusa-sēnaba, sons of Gaṅgādhara-
sēnabōva.

inscription.head:
No. 366.
(A.R. No. 329 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 4) set up in the inner prākāra of the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

The record registers a gift of land by Īśvara-sāvanta, younger
brother of Kōṭīsara-sāvanta for feeding brāhmaṇas in the satra attached
to the Mahādēva temple at Surāla, during the time of Ñāgañcha
Toḷaha when his younger brother Bemmaṇṇañcha was enjoying the
kumāra-vṛitti. It mentions Neṇagunda-30.

It is dated Śaka 137[4], Āṅgīrasa, Chaitra śu. 1, Wednesday
corresponding to 1452 A.D., March 22.
1. This is engraved in continuation of the above as a supplement.

inscription.head:
No. 367.
(A.R. No. 518 of 1928-29.)
DAREGUḌḌE, (NEAR KELLA PUTTIGE) KARKAL TALUK,
SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the prākāra of the Viṭṭhala (Sōmanāthēśvara) temple.

Barring the date Śaka [1376], Bhāva, no other details of this
badly damaged record can be made out. It corresponds to 1454-55 A.D.

l.note: The rest of the

inscription.head:
No. 368.
(A.R. No. 502 of 1928-29.)
PERDŪR, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of Vīrabhadra temple.

This record is damaged dated Śaka 1379, Dhātu, Tulā 12,
Monday corresponding to 1456 A.D., October 11. The Śaka year,
however, was current.

It registers a gift of land, by purchase, left in charge of the
eleven Janani and the Three hundred of the Brahmasabha, by
Basavaṇṇarasa alias Baṅgā, for burning two perpetual lamps in the
temple of Janārdanadēva of Peraḍūru, and for making a lamp-stand in
bronze.

inscription.head:
No. 369.
(A.R. No. 482 of 1928-29.)
UJRE, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of the Janārdanasvāmi temple.

The inscription states that Viṭharasa-oḍeya gave the village Ujiri
as uṁbaḷi to Kāmirāya-arasa and Dēvaṇṇa-Kōṭhāri as a compensation
for his (Viṭharasa's) having burnt their palace at Koḍeyāḷa and the
village Nirumārga, and records the order the latter gave to the
residents of that village regarding the payment of taxes which were to
be thereafter paid to him.

It is dated Śaka 1391, Virōdhi, Śrāvaṇa śu. 3, Tuesday
corresponding to 1469 A.D., July 11.

inscription.head:
No. 370.
(A.R. No. 478 of 1928-29.)
INDABETTU, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the deserted Vīrabhadra temple in a field in the village.

This is dated Śaka 1394, Vijaya, Kārttika śu. 15 corresponding
to 1473 A.D., November 4. The details are not verifiable. The Śaka
year is wrong for 1395.

It records a gift of land by Kāmi[rā]ya-arasa also called Baṅga, to
Nanjappa for worshipping god Vīrabhadradēva of Baṁgavāḍi. It was
stipulated that the land was to be enjoyed by the donee hereditarily
and if he did not haveany issues the gift land would be the property
of god Vīrabhadradēya.

[ka]ṁ bittuva muḍe tāḍe bayalu mu 25 beṁdu
lu nuṟumuḍe bhattavanu naḍasi ·ā devara dēvapūjeya-

inscription.head:
No. 371.
(A.R. No. 173 of 1933-34.)
RĀMASAMUDRAM, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North wall of Janārdanasvāmi temple.

It registers a gift of land situated below the tank at Rāmasamudram,
for offerings to god Janārdanadēva by Chikka Timmarāja, son of
Timmarāja, for the merit of Narasiṅgarāja-voḍeya.

It is dated Śaka 1401, Vikāri, Kārttika śu. 12, which details are
insufficient for vertification. The tithi corresponds to 1479 A.D.,
October 27.

inscription.head:
No. 372.
(A.R. No. 552 of 1929-30.)
UPPUNDA, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) set up in the prākāra of the Durgā-Paramēśvarī temple.

This damaged record registers a gift of land for the maintenance
of the piper playing during the śrībali service in the temple of Durgā-
Paramēśvarī at Uppugunda by Parasu-heggaḍe, nephew (? aḷiya) of
Aṇṇasāvanta.

It is dated Śaka 1405, Śubhakṛit, [Kārttika śu. 5, Thursday],
corresponding to 1482 A.D., October 17.

inscription.head:
No. 373.
(A.R. No. 481 of 1928-29.)
GURUVĀYANAKERE, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up near the steps leading to the south prākāra of
Śāntīśvara-basti.

This badly damaged record seems to register a gift to the
Kannaḍi-basti probably by Nārasiṁha-banga.
It is dated Śaka 1406 corresponding to 1484-85 A.D.

l.note: The

inscription.head:
No. 374.
(A.R. No. 582 of 1929-30.)
BAḌAGABEṬṬU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab lying by the side of the tank belonging to the private house
called ‘Chitpāḍi-bīḍu’.

The record registers a gift of land for feeding the brahmaṇas in
the maṭha at Chiṭṭapāḍi by Jinabhakta-Dēvaru-seṭṭi who purchased the
land from Lakshmīnārāyaṇa-mūḍila for that purpose.

It is dated Śaka 1408, Viśvāvasu, Bhādrapada śu. 12, Tuesday
corresponding to 1485 A.D., August 23. The Śaka year was current.

inscription.head:
No. 375.
(A.R. No. 523 of 1928-29.)
NĀRĀVI, KARKALA TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the prākāra of the Sūryanārāyaṇasvāmin temple.

This is dated Śaka 14[1]1, Saumya, Mīna 1, corresponding to
1490 A.D., February 25. The details given, however, are not verifiable.

It records a gift of land for offerings to god Sūryanārāyaṇadēva
of Nārāvi by Kāmādēvi, mother of Maṇḍalika Sōmanātha Peṁnnaṁṇṇa-
arasa.

l.ka: vē | svasti śrīmatu śakavaruṣa 14[1]1 neya saumya
mā dēviyaru māḍida dharma nārāviya sūryya
·aṟigadde 2 kkaṁ bha mū • 6 mattaṁ be • ṁ beṭṭina • •

inscription.head:
No. 376.
(A.R. No. 347 of 1930-31.)
BAḶPA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the ruined Durgā temple.

This badly damaged record registers a gift by Dēvarāya-voḍeya.
But all details are lost.

It is dated the cyclic year Pramādīcha, Chaitra, śu. 9, Tuesday.
Palaeographically the record may be assigned to the 15th century in
which case the details correspond to 1493 A.D., March 26 which might
be date intended.

inscription.head:
No. 377.
(A.R. No. 581 of 1929-30.)
BAḌAGABEṬṬU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab built into the floor of the cow-shed in a private house called
‘chitpāḍi-bīḍu.’

This badly damaged worn out record is dated Śaka 1416,
Pramādicha, Mēsha 1, Sunday. The details are irregular, the tithi
corresponding to 1493 A.D., March 27, the weekday being Wednesday.
The Śaka year was 1415.

It registers a gift of land by a person (name lost) who was the
grand daughter's son of Devaru-seṭṭi, for feeding a brahmaṇa probably
in the maṭha attached to the temple of Lakshmīnārāyaṇa at Chiṭṭapāḍi.
The details are lost. Cf. No. 582/29-30 above.

mūḍalu mukkaṇa gadde yi • • • ḍuvalu teṁkalu sā-

inscription.head:
No. 378.
(A.R. No. 156 of 1933-34.)
KURIJILI, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South side of the boulder near the Veṅkaṭēśvarasvāmin temple.

This record registers a gift of land, situated in Guṇḍalahaḷḷi alias
Narasihapura in Hode-nāḍu, as Kaṭṭugoḍage by Dēvapa, son of
Koṇḍapa Timmaṇa, the sthānika of the Nārasihadēva temple, to
Nārasihadēva, son of Alapa for digging a tank in the village which
belonged to the temple of Kadiri Lakshmīnarasiṁha and bringing the
surrounding lands under cultivation. The land was endowed under
certain conditions in respect of the rate of produce to be paid by the
tenant. A portion of the land was also given to the same person as
dasavanda.

It is dated Śaka 1419, Naḷa, Vaiśākha ba. 10 corresponding to
1496 A.D., May 10, the Śaka year being current. But the details are
not verifiable.

l.ka:
śubhamastu [|*] keṟeya kaṭugoḍageya sādhanada prati [|*] svastiśrī

inscription.head:
No. 379.
(A.R. No. 483 of 1928-29.)
SURIYA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pedestal of the Nandi in front of the Sadāśiva temple.

This record in characters of about the 15th century, is dated the
cyclic year Piṅgala, Mithuna 17, Sunday. It states that the image of
Nandikēśvara was set up in the temple at Sureya by Nārāyaṇa
Sēnabōva, son of Saṅkuvadhikāri of Nūje, in the reign of Vīra
Narasiṅga-Lakshmapparasa alias Baṅgarāja Voḍeya. As this chief was
ruling in the latter half of the 15th century, the cyclic year mentioned
in the record may be equated with 1497 A.D. But the other details
are irregular as the given tithi corresponded to June 14 of that year
when the weekday was Wednesday.

l.ka:
piṁgala saṁvatsarada mithuna msa 17 ne ravivāradallū śrīmatu
vīranarasiṁga lakṣmapparsarāda baṁgarāja voḍēra kāladalli nūjeya

saṁkuvadhikārimaga nārāyaṇa sēnabōvanu sureyada [śrī]dēvara
saṁnidhiyalli pratiṣṭemāḍida naṁdikēśvaranu naḍasida saṁbu[gai]ddu
śubhamastu śrī [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 380.
(A.R. No. 364 of 1930-31.)
MAṬṬU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the Vishṇumūrti temple.

This is dated Śaka 1421, Siddhārthi, Kārttika-paurṇami,
corresponding to 1499 A.D., October 19. The details, however, cannot
be verified.

It registers a gift of money income and probably also some land
to god Kōṭinātha by Basavarasa-voḍeya who appears to have got the
temple repaired, when he was ruling Tuḷu-rājya. As the record is
damaged, the details are lost.

l.ka: ḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu yiṁtī catussīme yiṁdaṁ voḷaguḷa •
• • • • • • • • • • ḷa kāṭi ga 416 ya • • • saha nidhinikṣēpa

inscription.head:
No. 381.
(A.R. No. 466 of 1928-29.)
TIRUVAILU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in a field called prākāra Bākimār about a mile to the
north of Amṛitēśvara temple.

This badly damaged record registers a gift of land, the details of
which are all lost.

l.note: The rest of

inscription.head:
No. 382.
(A.R. No. 510 of 1928-29.)
KŌTE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 4.) in the prākāra of the Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.
This record is very badly damaged and nothing can be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 383.
(A.R. No. 532 of 1928-29.)
NALLŪRU, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the south prākāra of the Pārśvanātha basti.

This record registers a gift of two mūḍe of rice for each
abhishēka of Pārśvanātha at Naluru, by Sālva.

inscription.head:
No. 384.
(A.R. No. 544 of 1928-29.)
BAINDŪRU, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab built into the well of a private house near the tank called
‘Hosakere’.

This record registers a gift of land in Yeḍatoṟe, after purchase
for feeding brahmaṇas in the maṭha built by him, by Vīrūseṭṭi, the
younger brother of Bemmaṇāseṭṭi.

It is dated the cyclic year Vikṛita Chaitra śu. 1 the details
being insufficient for verification in the absence of the mention of
Śaka year and the weekday.

inscription.head:
No. 385.
(A.R. No. 578 of 1928-29.)
PUTTŪR, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying near the tank in the quarter called ‘Māyaguṇḍi’

This badly damaged record is dated the cyclic year Tāraṇa
Vriśchika, other being lost. It mentions Puttūru and seems to register a
gift of money to the grāma-dēvaru.

l.note: The rest of the record

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(A.R. No. 592 of 1929-30.)
KUMRUGŌḌU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab in a field about three furlongs to the south of the
Śaṅkaranārāyaṇa temple.

This badly damaged Kannaḍa record engraved in Nāgarī script
refers to a gift of land the details of which, including the date, are all
lost.

l.de: • • • gaḍiyiṁda mūḍalu • • • • • yiṁdaṁ teṁkalu yiṁti catussīmeyoḷage • • •

inscription.head:
No. 387.
(A.R. No. 109 of 1930-31.)
TIRUPPUNDURUTTI, TANJORE TALUK, TANJORE DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Stone built into the south wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the
central shrine of the Pushpavanēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record is dated the cyclic year Prabhava
Kārttika śu. 5. The details cannot to verified, It mentions Tagaḍūru
and Mallikārjuna.

inscription.head:
No. 388.
(A.R. No. 339 of 1930-31.)
SĪMANTŪRU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two sides of a slab set up in the Janārdanasvāmi temple.

This badly damaged record refers to an agreement between two
parties defining their rights on the land etc.

inscription.head:
No. 386.
(A.R. No. 254 of 1931-32.)
KACHCHŪRU, (KŌTAKĒRI) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Broken slab (No. 4) lying near the hōmakuṇḍa in the
Pañchaliṅgēśvara temple.

This record, in characters of about the 15th century, registers a
grant of money to Haṁparasa by Viṭharasa-oḍeya. It refers to
Kōṭeyakēri and to god Pañchaliṅgadēva. Karaṇika Mallarasa of Bārakūru
wrote the record.

inscription.head:
No. 390.
(A.R. No. 264 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Broken slab set up near the field of Baḍatibayala-seṭṭi on the road to
Chauḷikēri.

This damaged record seems to refer to an agreement. It refers to
the Hattukēri of Bārakūru and to Nārāyaṇadēva.

l.note: The inscription is

inscription.head:
No. 391.
(A.R. No. 268 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab set up in Yeḍatāruseṭṭi's hāḍe (jungle) at Chauḷikēri.

This fragmentary record registers a gift of land, made after
purchase, to god Gōpīnathadēva by some persons. The details are all,
however, lost.

l.ka:

inscription.head:
No. 392.
(A.R. No. 273 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the Jōgi-maṇḍapa at Chauḷikēri.

This damaged record registers a gift of land to the chhatra
(attached to the maṭha of Anupamaṇātha-voḍeya, by several seṭṭis. It is
dated the cyclic year Akshaya, Bhāra 25. But the details given are
not sufficient for verification.

inscription.head:
No. 393.
(A.R. No. 275 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀḶA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying in the garden adjoining the tank at Chauḷikēri.

This badly damaged fragmentary record refers to gift of land the
details of which are all lost.

l.note: The

inscription.head:
No. 394.
(A.R. No. 277 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀḶA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying on the aśvatthakaṭṭe in front of the Vināyaka temple.

This mutilated record registers a gift of land by Basavaseṭṭi for
feeding brāhmaṇas in the feeding house attached to a maṭha. The gift
was entrusted to Krishṇa-hebbāra, son of Mādaṇa-hebbāra. It refers to
god Gōpīnāthadēva.
