inscription.head:
No. 395.
(A.R. No. 279 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀḶA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab lying near the well in the garden opposite the
Vināyaka temple at Chauḷikēri.

This badly changed record seems to register a gift of land and
money, the details of which are all lost.

inscription.head:
No. 396.
(A.R. No. 280 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀḶA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab lying in Puṭṭaṇṇayya's garden at Chauḷikēri.

This badly damaged record registers a gift of land and money to
god Gōpīnāthadēva. The details of the grant are all lost.

inscription.head:
No. 397.
(A.R. No. 289 of 1931-32.)
HĒRĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) set up in the temple of Janārdanadēva.

This record is engraved on both sides of the slab the first side
of which is badly damaged. The name of the king and of his
subordinates are therefore lost. The extant portion refers to the year 75
the first two digits of which are lost. It registers gifts of land and
money for offerings etc, in a temple and for feeding brāhmaṇas.

inscription.head:
No. 398.
(A.R. No. 300 of 1931-32.)
KĀVAḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab standing in the Gaṇapati temple.

This fragmentary record registers a gift of land, the boundaries
of which alone are available from the extant portion.

inscription.head:
No. 399.
(A.R. No. 306 of 1931-32.)
KAKKUÑJE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Broken slab built into east wall of the central shrine of
Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This multilated record registers a gift of land to a maṭha and for
conducting Rudra-pūje in the temple of Māhādēva, Saṅkaradēva. The
gift was entrusted to Kariya Lakhaṇṇa.

edition.h4: TEXT. The beginning of the record is lost.

inscription.head:
No. 400.
(A.R. No. 307 of 1931-32.)
KAKKUÑJE, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Broken slab (No. 2) built into east wall of the central shrine of
Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This fragmentary record refers to a gift of land, money and oil,
probably made to a god, the details of which are all lost. It refers to
god Mahāliṅga.

edition.h4: TEXT. The beginning of the record is broken and lost.

inscription.head:
No. 401.
(A.R. No. 326 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 1) set up in the inner prākāra of Mahāliṅgēśvara temple.

This record, badly damaged in the beginning, registers a gift of
land to god Mahādēva by Aṇṇa Kalukūra. Iśvari, daughter of
Mudavariya Rāma was entrusted to fetch the rice to the temple.
Bemmaṇañcha Toḷaha is referred to as one of the witnesses to the gift.

inscription.head:
No. 402.
(A.R. No. 338 of 1931-32.)
KEÑJŪRU, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken slab set up in the temple of Abbaṇaśeṭṭi Gōpālakṛishṇadēva.

This mutilated record registers gifts of lands etc. probably to god
Kṛishṇadēva by several individuals. It refers to the two villages
Keñjūru and Cherppaḷḷi and to Bemmaṇañcha Toḷaha. All other details
are lost.

No. 403.
(A.R. No. 52 of 1938-39.)
ŚRĪRAṄGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK, TRICHINOPOLY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the second prākāra of the Raṅganāthaswamy temple.

It record the name of several female individuals who all
probably pilgrims, belonged to the royal retinue.

inscription.head:
No. 404.
(A.R. No. 304 of 1931-32.)
ŚIRIYĀRA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing in Teṅka-bayalu.

This badly damaged record in characters of about the 15th-16th
century registers gifts of land gold Narasiṁha. It refers to a certain
dēvarasa. All other details are lost.

l.ka:
daka dāna dhārā pūrvvakavāgi sarvvamāṁnyavāki dhāreyaneṟadu
kocevāgi yi

inscription.head:
No. 405.
(A.R. No. 593 of 1929-30.)
HANDĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab built into a sluice in a field called Kambaḷagadde.

This record registers a gift of land for feeding brahmaṇas in the
Śaṅkaranārāyaṇadēva temple (of Koḍage ?), by Sōmaṇaya, younger
brother of Basavarasa-oḍeya and son of Mallarasa-oḍeya of Hārabhaṇḍi,
for the merit of Narasaṇa-oḍeya. It states that the gift land was
originally granted to acertain Chandaṇṇa by Dēvarāya-mahārāja.

It certains two dates, viz., (1) Śaka 1424 (1425 current),
Dundubhi, Kārttika śu. 5 and (2) Āsvija ba. 30, Monday, solar
eclipse obviously of the year same year. The weekday was, however,
Saturday. The latter probably was the date of gift while the former,
the details of which are not verifiable, but corresponds to 1502 A.D.,
October 6, was the date when the record was put on stone.

inscription.head:
No. 406.
(A.R. No. 23 of 1930-38.)
ŚRĪRAṄGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK, TRICHINOPOLY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the second prākāra of the Raṅganāthaswami temple.

This record registers a gift of land made after purchase for 25
varāhas, for supplying garlands, coconuts, betel-leaves etc., to the god
by Chikka Narasaṇa-nāyaka, son of a nāyaka.

It is dated the cyclic year Dundubhi, Kārttika śu. 15, Wednesday,
lunar eclipse. On palaeographical grounds it may be assigned to about
the 15th century. The details probably correspond to 1502 A.D.,
October 15, when the lunar eclipse occurred a Saturday.

inscription.head:
No. 407.
(A.R. No. 157 of 1933-34.)
KURIJILI, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: North side of the boulder near the Veṇkatēśvara temple.

This record is a lease-deed of lands situated in the village of
Guṇḍlahaḷi belonging to the temple of Kadiri Nārasiṁhadēva of
Muḷuvāgila, excluding from them the area given as dasavanda, made
ever by Viṭhaya and Kuppaya, son-in-law of Anantappa, the sthānikas
of the temple, to Nārasiṁhadēva of Rāmasamudra.

It is dated the cyclic year Rudhirōdgārin, Jyēshṭha ba. 12,
Wednesday. On palaeographical grounds, the record may be assigned to
the 16th century when the details regularly correspond to 1503 A.D.,
June 21, which appears to be the intended date.

inscription.head:
No. 408.
(A.R. No. 480 of 1928-29.)
GURUVĀYANAKERE, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
East tier of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the
Śāntīśvara-basti.

This record with the beginning of each of the lines lost, is dated
Śaka 1443, Śukla Māgha ba. 10, Monday. The cyclic year given
corresponded to Śaka 1431, when the other details regularly correspond
to 1530 A.D., February 4.

It records gifts of land garden with the consent of the residents
of Beḷataṁgaḍi. All other details are lost.

inscription.head:
No. 409.
(A.R. No. 543 of 1929-30.)
BAINDŪR, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab built into the compound wall of a private house near the tank
called ‘Hosakere’

This damaged record is dated Śaka 1435, Śrīmukha, Vaiśākha,
the other details being lost. The year corresponds to 1513-14 A.D.

It registers a gift of land, the boundaries of which are given; but
the details are all lost. It refers to the rule of some king bearing the
tiles rājādhirāja and paramēśvara.

inscription.head:
No. 410.
(A.R. No. 322 of 1931-32.)
KUDI, (No. 34) UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab standing near the temple of Dēvayya-seṭṭi Gōpālakṛishṇadēva.

This badly damaged record refers to the construction of a maṭha
and a gift land to the same probably by Kōṭisāvanta Toḷaha and
Saṇkama-heggaḍe. Details are all lost.

It is dated Śaka 1446, (1447 current), Tāraṇa śu. 13, Thursday,
corresponding to 1524 A.D., July 13 when the week-day was
Wednesday.

l.ka: ppe yi maṭhada sarvvasā • sarvvamānya dānapālanayō-

inscription.head:
No. 411.
(A.R. No. 522 of 1928-29.)
NELLIKARA, KERAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab set up in the east verandah of the prākāra of the
Anantanātha-basti.

This record is dated Śaka 1447, Tāraṇa, Dhanus 15, Sunday.
The details given are irregular, to 1524 A.D., December 13 when the
weekday was Tuesday.

It register a gift of paddy from the land presented by her
younger brother Dēvaṁṇarasa alias Komtu, to the basti at Kāyaravura,
by Śaṁkara-dēvi. Dēvara-senabōva wrote the record.

l.ka: ya bhta ba 30 le mu 60 nū ka śaṁkha-
vaṁ vi • va • vaṇege sāryya caṁdrara

inscription.head:
No. 412.
(A.R. No. 517 of 1928-29.)
DAREGUḌḌE, (NEAR KELLA PUTTIGE), KARKAL TALUK,
SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Sūryanārāyaṇa temple.

This badly damaged record is dated Śaka 1447 Vyaya, Vṛishabha
15, Sunday corresponding to 1526 A.D., May 12 when the weekday
was Saturday and the Śaka year was 1448.

It seems to register a gift of money among others probably by
Koṁnara alias Koṁtu-arasa.

inscription.head:
No. 413.
(A.R. No. 336 of 1930-31.)
SUJĒRU, MANGALORE TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying about two furlongs from Bhūtasthāna temple.

This record is dated Śaka 1450 Saravadhāri Viśākha ba. 2,
Tuesday corresponding to 1528 A.D., May 5, Tuesday, f.d.t. ·34.

It registers an agreement defining the rights and privileges of
Tuḷuvarasa alias Chauṭa of Puttige and the Aḷi-sāvira and Baḷi-sāvira
on one side, and Vira-Narasiṁha Baṅga and his 5000 followers on the
others. The agreement was arrived at through the arbitration of
Vēdānanda-voḍeya, disciple of Kṛisṇānanda-voḍeya and in the presence
of Tirumalarasa alias Kinnika-heggaḍe.

inscription.head:
No. 414.
(A.R. No. 524 of 1928-29.)
SHIRTĀḌI, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of Baidarkal Bhūtanātha.

This record is dated Śaka 1453 Vikṛiti, Mēśa 15, Sunday
corresponding to 1530 A.D., April 11 when the Śaka year was current
and the weekday Monday.

It states that Dēvaṇṇa-arasa alias Koṁna got a new temple (of
Sōmanātha 7) constructed at Ārjunāpura and made a gift of 75
varāha-gadyāṇas to Koṭyaṇa-āḷva for conducting worship in the
temple. It also records a gift of tax-free land to the deity by
Chennarāja-oḍeya. Ramasēnabōva wrote the record.

l.ka: la sāśa[na*]da krama ·eṁtiyādare kalliṭṭamūḍe nāḍi bāḷisi

inscription.head:
No. 415.
(A.R. No. 158 of 1933-34.)
TIRUMALAREḌḌIPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Two pieces of a broken slab lying in the Gaṇgamma shrine

This registers a gift of eight koḷaga of land to Mācha Ma[luga],
son of Golapāpa by mahāmaṇḍalēśvara Timmarāja, son of Viruparāja,
for supplying water in the water-shed maintained by him at the fair
(saṅte) in Kadiri. The gift land was situated in Chemmūru which
belonged to the nāyakatana of the donor.

It is dated Śaka 14[62], Śā[rvvari], śu. 15, the name of the
month not being clear. The year corresponds to 1540-41 A.D.

inscription.head:
No. 416.
(A.R. No. 565 of 1929-30.)
HATYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 3) set up in the prākāra of Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

It is dated Śaka 1462, Viśvāvasu. The cyclic year corresponds
with Śaka 1467 (1545-46 A.D.)

The record specifies the amount of paddy to be supplied to the
Lōkanāthadēva temple from the lands, whose boundaries etc. are given,
situated at Gulavāḍi. It also registers two other gifts of land made to
the same temple respectively by Nandiyāḷuva and Nāraṇa, grandsons of
Dāruseṭṭi-gaḷutti and by Mallayya-mugila, son of Nāraṇa-hegaḍe. The
first of those two gifts was made in the year Īśvara, Bhādrapada śu. 1,
Tuesday.

The second gift was made in the summer month of the cyclic
year Piṅgaḷa.

inscription.head:
No. 417.
(A.R. No. 363 of 1930-31.)
TRASI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Hero-stone set up by the road side in the village.

This damaged record is stated to have been set up by Kiri-tāyi
Basava-nāyakiti probably in memory of the death of a hero. It refers to
Honnādēvi-amma, the ruler of the manneya of Hāḍuvali and to the
bhanḍāri (treasury) of Saṅkaradēvi-amma. It is dated Śaka 1462,
Viśvāvasu. The cyclic year corresponds with Śaka 1467 (1545-46 A.D.)

It is dated Śaka 1468, Parābhava, Kārttika ba. 1, Vaḍḍavāra.
The tithi corresponds to 1546 A.D., November 8, the weekday being
Monday.

ka.b:
• • • • • śālivāhana śakavaruśa 1498 saṁdu vartamāna parābhava
saṁvatsa

l.ka:
rada kārtika ba 1 vaḍavāradalu hāḍuvaḷiya manneya ·arasarāda
hoṁnādēvi ·aṁmana
• • • ·āḷuva yirida kāladi • hāyidu hāyisi koṁdu • • manadaṇiya

inscription.head:
No. 418.
(A.R. No. 516 of 1928-29.)
MSNTRĀDI, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the compound of Konārarāja's palace.

This badly damaged record, the details of cohich are lost, registers
a gift of money and paddy to god Sōmanāth. It is dated the cyclic
year Prāmādīcha, Mīna 30, Paurnami Monday. The record is in
characters of about the 16th century. The intended date corresponding
to the details given might be 1554 A.D. March 19, Monday, Paurnami
but the day of the solar month Mina was 22 and not 30.

inscription.head:
No. 419.
(A.R. No. 564 of 1929-30.)
HATYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) set up in the prākāra of the Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

This is dated Śaka 1492, Śukla, Jyēshṭha ba. 6, Monday. The
details are irregular, the tithi corresponding to 1569 A.D., June 4.

It records a gift of land in Hoḷamage of Halasunāḍa-sīṁe by
Baṁkiyarasa alias Ammidēvi-amma to Śaṅkaranārāyaṇa-bhaṭṭa, son of
Bāḷēya Muruḍēśvara-bhaṭṭa of Basarūru, who in turn, gifted the same
to the Uppaukallu-maṭha of the temple of Lōkanāthadēva of Haṭṭiyaṅgaḍi.

inscription.head:
No. 420.
(A.R. No. 353 of 1930-31.)
VIṬLA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone water-cistern by the road-side.

This record dated, Śaka 1493, Prajōtpatti Ve. śu. 5, Sunday,
states that the water-trough was set up by Kuuṁñidēvarasa alias
Doṁba-heggaḍe to secure merit. The details of date correspond to 1571
A.D., April 29, the month obviously being Vaiśākha.

l.ka:
śrīsvasti namaḥstuṁga śiraścuṁbita caṁdracāmara cāravē trailōkya
nagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē

svastiśrī vijayādbhudaya śālivāhana ṣakavaruṣa 1493 saṁduvartamāna
prajōtpatya saṁvatsarada ve śudha 5 ravivāradallu śrīma-

tu viṭhalada kuñi dēvarasarāda doṁbahegaḍeyaru tamage puṁṇyavāga
bēkeṁdu gō [u] gaḷu ·udakavakoṁba • • ḍisi hākidakallu • •

giyanu tegadukoṁḍu hōdavarige kāśiyali sāvira kapileyaṁ koṁda
pāpaveṁdu baradasāsanāṁkita • śubhamastu

inscription.head:
No. 421.
(A.R. No. 79 of 1944-45.)
BANNIHAṬṬI, HOSPET TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Hanumān temple.

This fragmentary record refers to Śaka 149[5], Āṅgi[rasa*], all
other details being lost. The Śaka year corresponds to 1572-73 A.D.,
being current.

inscription.head:
No. 422.
(A.R. No. 563 of 1929-30.)
HATYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) set up in the prākāra of the Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

This record engraved below No. 423, confirms a gift of land to
the god Lōkanāthadēva made by the two brothers Duggaṇa-Koṅga and
Timmappa Koṅga and by Baṁkiyarasa alias Hoṅnaya-Kaṁbaḷi-voḍeya
alias Ammidēvi-amma. The two donors had inherited the gift land
from their grand-mother Sūramma-seṭṭi. The latter had received the
same in the year Plavaṅga as kanyādāna from her grand-father Hiriya-
Hoṁneya-Kaṁbaḷi-voḍeya alias Baṁki-arasa who, for that purpose,
purchased the land from Yisara-aḍah, son of Kōṭīsara-aḍaha for 300
varāha-gadyāṇas.

It is dated Śaka 1498, Yuva, Vaiśākha-amāvāsye, Tuesday
corresponding to 1575 A.D., May 10. The Śaka year was current.

l.ka:

kramaveṁteṁdare hiriya hoṁne-

·asabhasabha baki keḷagaṇa teṁgina tōṭamuṁtāda sarvvasvakke baṁkiyarasaru
honnaya kaṁbaḷi voḍeyaru kōṭēsara ·aḍahana maga yisara ·aḍahage
koṭa varaha ga 300 ·akṣaradallūyida

muṁnūrru varahananū koṭṭu voḷagaṇa mūla horragaṇa ·aruvāra
saha biḍāruvāravāgi biḍisikoṁḍu naṁma voṁmagaḷu hoṁnnamma
seṭṭitige kaṁnyā dānavāgi yo sarvvasvada ·a-

rtha baṁdaru ·urattibaṁdaru ·ākegeyeṁdu koṭṭa patra yi patrakke ·āda
ārrikeya hoṇe mukalāṭiya yisara·aḍaharu sakṣiya doregaḷu
hoṁnnayyameraṭa koṭaṇa kaṁba-

ḷi maṇiheggeḍeyaru yiṁtivaru bhayāṁnumatadiṁda karttanavoppa
heṇekārrana voppa ciṭṭimakkiya brahmaru mūliya voppa kōṭada
ayirōḍiya braṁharu

sākṣi doregaḷa voppa | yo sarvvasvavanū hoṁnama seṭṭiyatiyu ·avara
saṁtānadavaru baddu baralāgi ·ā sūraṁma seṭatiya mūṁmakaḷu dugaṇa
koṁganu tiṁmappa koṁganu yi kartaru vo-

ppi sūryagrahaṇadali haṭṭi yaṁgaḍi lōkanātha dēvarige ·amṛtapaḍige
yi bayalu makki saha mu 16 || hadinārrumuḍiyu mūvattu hāne
gaddeyanū dhāreya nerradu ko

ṭṭadu nāu yi sarvasvavanu voppidēvarige koṭṭe·u yi sarvada ·artha
baṁdaru ·urattibaṁdaru dēvarabhaṁḍhārakke ·agumāḍikoṁḍu baddu bahadu
| muṁde yo sarvasvavanū hiridu

hoṁneya kaṁbaḷivoḍeyaru sūraṁma seṭṭitiya tāyi hoṁnamaseṭitiga

dharmasādhanada paṭṭi yidakke sākṣiga |

inscription.head:
No. 423.
(A.R. No. 562 of 1929-30.)
HATYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 2) set up in prākāra of the Lokanāthēśvara temple.

This record registers a gift god several pieces of land to god
Lōkanāthadēva of Hattiyaṅgaḍi by the seṭṭikāras of that place. It states
that Baṅkiyarasa alias Hoṁneya-Kaṁbaḷi-voḍeya alias Ammidēvi-amma,
Chenneyarasa Toḷaha and the kings of Yikkare (Ikkērī) were ruling
Hārunāḍa-sīme as kāṇāchi.

It is dated Śaka 1499, Dhātu, Chaitra śu. 1, the details being
insufficient for verification in the absence of the mention of weekday.
The tithi corresponds to 1576 A.D., March 1, the Śaka year being
current.

inscription.head:
No. 424.
(A.R. No. 558 of 1929-30.)
HATTIYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in prākāra of the Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

This record is dated Śaka 1499, Īśvara, Chaitra śu. 15, Tuesday
corresponding to 1577 A.D., April 2.

It registers a gift of income from taxes like bēḍige birāḍa etc.,
levied on the dēvasva lands of the deities in Guḷavāḍi of Hārū-nāḍu
and those of god Kōṭiśvara of Kuḍakūru, to god Lōkanātha of
Haṭṭiyaṅgaḍi for conducting regular worship according to the period, by
Baṁkiyarasa alias Honneya Kaṁbaḷi-voḍeya.

l.ka:
śiraścuṁbi caṁdracāmaracāravē trayilōkya nagarāraṁbha mūlastaṁbhāya
śaṁbhavē svastiśrī vijayā-

dbhudaya śālivāhana śakavaru[ṣa] 1499 saṁdu ·upari saluva
vartamāna yo[śva]ra saṁvatsarada cayitraśuddha

15 maṁgaḷavāradallū śrīmatu haṭṭayaṁgaḍiya lōkanāthadēvara
divyaśrī pādapadmaṁgaḷige baṁkiyarasarā[da]

hoṁneyya kaṁbaḷi voḍeyaru[vo]ḍavaṭu koṭa sahiraṇyōdaka
dānadhārāpūrvakada dānapaṭṭiya va[kha]ṇeya

kramaveṁteṁdare naṁma hā[ru*]nāḍasīmeya guḷavāḍiya maṁdinali
dēvara dēvaśvagaḷige saluva bhūmiyamē-

le kuḍukūra kōṭīśvaradēvara dēvaśvada bhūmigaḷamēlu kā[la]kālaṁ
pratiyalū naṁma ·aramanege bēḍiga birā-

·anayasaya ·akara muṁtāda haṇahoṁnugaḷaṁnnu hiṁde kālakālaṁ
pratiyalu naṁma ·aramane-

ge tegadukoṁḍu barutayidevāgi [ā] haṇa hoṁnu yēnuṁṭādarū
kuḍukura kōṭīśvaradēvara dēvastānakke

naḍava ·aramaneya vartaneya haṇamaṁtādudanu sahavāgi kālakālaṁ
pratiyallū naḍavaṁtā ·ayana

viniyōgada veccagaḷigāgi ·ahahāṁge tathātithiya dinada sōmēpparāga
puṁṇyakāladali dēvara śrīpāda-

ke sahiraṇyōdaka dāna dhārāpūrvakavāgi dhāreya neradu koṭṭe·u
yosaṁmaṁdada haṇa hoṁnu

yēnuṁ[ṭā]dadanu [hā] ru nāḍanāḍu biṭṭu hasaraṇeya mēlaṇa bēḍige
birā[ḍa] yēnuṁ[ṭṭā]dadanu kālaṁ

duyeṁdu koṭa sahiraṁṇyōdaka dāna dhārāpūrvakada sādana [||*] dāna
pālanayōrmadyē dānāśrēyōnu pālanaṁ

dānaṁ svargamavāpyōti pālanādacyataṁ padaṁ | svadattaṁ dviguṇaṁ
puṁṇyaṁ | paradattānu pālanaṁ paradattāpahārē-

ṇa svadattāṁ niśchalaṁ bhavētu | yiṁ [tu]dakke sākṣigaḷu ·ādityacaṁdrāvanilānala
uca dyaurbhūmirāpō[hru]-

dayaṁ yamaśca ·ahaśca rātriśca ·ubhēca saṁdyē darṁmmasya jānāti
narasyarittaṁ [||*] baṁkkarasaru baraha [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 425.
(A.R. No. 559 of 1929-30.)
HATTIYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in prākāra of the Lokanāthēśvara temple.

This record is dated Śaka 1499, Īśvara, bēsage (summer season)
27, Friday. The actual month is not given. The months Jyēshṭha-
Āshāḍha being the summer months, when the given details prabably
correspond to 1577 A.D., May 24 which seems to be the intended
date.

The record ratifies the gift of income from taxes made to god
Lōkanātha of Haṭṭiyaṅgaḍi by Baṁkiyarasa alias Honne Kaṁbaḷi-
voḍeya, by the nāḍu, dēśa and all other residents of Hārū-nāḍu.

l.ka:
svastiśrī vijayodbudeya śālivāhana śakavaruśa 1499 saṁdu saluva
vartamānayośvara saṁcyarada bē

sageya tiṁgaḷu 27 hōha śukravāradalu śrīmattu haṭṭiyaṁgaḍiya
lōkanāthadēvara divyaśrīpādapa-

dmaṁgaḷige dēvarabhaṁḍārake hārunāḍa nāḍudēśa samastaru saduvineya
bhayabhaṁktiyiṁ śāṣṭāṁga veragi

poḍavaṁṭṭu barasikoṭa darṁmasādanada vakhaṇeya kramaveṁteṁdare naṁma
hārunāḍa guḷapāḍiya

maṁ[ḍi]navoḷage haṭṭiyaṁgaḍiya lōkanāthadēvara dēvasvada bāḷa [mē]
lu kuḍukūra koṭīśvaradēvaradē-

vasvadamēlu nānānāḍu • • • • • • • • • • birāḍa yēnuṁṭādadanu nāu
nāḍu

dēśa samastaru ta • • • • • • • • • • ·urnayiya ·ayana ·uścahakke dēvasvada
mēlebaṁda honu ha-

ṇa yēnuṁṭādudanu ·ācaṁdrārkkavāgi naḍadu bahaṁtāgi baṁkka ·arasaru
hone kaṁbaḷi voḍeyaru

biṭṭa dharmasādanakke [nā]u nāḍudēśa samastaru voppi barasi koṭṭe·u
yiṁ • • • sākṣigaḷu ·ādityacaṁdraru

nāḍudēśa samastara voppage • • • koṁḍada • • • [ūravaru] nā[ga] sēnabō
vana seṭṭiya baraha [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 426.
(A.R. No. 561 of 1929-30.)
HATTIYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in prākāra of the Lokanāthēśvara temple.

This is dated the cyclic year Bahudhānya, Bhādrapada ba. 8. It
is engraved below No. 427 below and is in characters of the 16th
century. Hence the date may be 1578 A.D., August 25. But the details
given are insufficient for verification.

It records gift of land in Haṭiya-aṅgaḍi in Hārunāḍa-sīme,
belonging to the palace, to god Lōkanātha of the place by Baṁkiyarasa
alias Honneya Kaṁbaḷi.

l.ka:
bahudhānya saṁvatsarada bhādrapada bahuḷa [8] lu śrīmatu
haṭiṁagaḍiya lōkanāta dēvara

bha[ṁ*] ḍarake baṁkiyarasarāda hoṁneya kaṁbaḷiyara [vo]ḍavaṭu darṁma
sādhanada biṁnavattaḷeya vakha-

ṇeya kramavetteṁdare naṁma hārunāḍa sīmeya haṭiya ·agaṁḍiyalu
aramane guḍe cattusīme vi-

vara mūḍalu yelusavadana gaḍiyiṁda paḍuvalu teṁkalu lōkanātha
dēvara dēvasada gaḍiyiṁdaṁ baḍagalu

paḍuvalu karuccara baḷiyavara bakiṭṭiti gaḍiyiṁdaṁ mūḍalu baḍagalu
aṁbaḷiyavara gaḍiyiṁdaṁ

teṁkalu yiṁtī nālku gaḍiyiṁdoḷage ·aramaneya hakkala guḍeyanu
dēvara dēvasvake dhāreya neradu ko-

ṭe·u yi taḷavanu ·agumāḍi kūḷacittaisudi sārrava mānyetaḷavanukoṭa
dhārmmasādanada binnavataḷi-

ya paṭi yittappudakke sakṣigaḷu ·āditya ca[ṁ*]draru [|*] ba[ṁ*]kkiyarasara
voppu [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 427.
(A.R. No. 560 of 1929-30.)
HATTIYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in prākāra of the Lokanāthēśvara temple.

This record is dated the cyclic year Bahudhānya, Bhādrapada
Kārttika, Saṅkarānti. As it is engraved on the same stone as No. 426
above and is in characters of the 16th century the year may be
equated to 1578-79 A.D., and the details of date to 1578 A.D., November
29, which was the day of Saṅkrānti in Karthika.

It registers gift of land free from taxes to god Lōkanātha of the
Haṭṭiyaṅgaḍi by Saṁkami, daughter of Bāurāutta who purchased the
land and presented it to his daughter for that purpose.

l.ka:
svasti bahudhānya saṁvatsarada kruttike saṁkṛti modalāgi śrīmatu
haṭṭiyaṁgaḍiya lōkanāthadēvara bha[ṁ]-

ḍārake bāurāuttara magaḷu sa[ṁ*]kami koṭṭa dharmasādanada patrada
vokhaṇekra[ma*] veṁteṁdare sābaladi

ya voḷage bāurāuttaru bōḷikēriya ·ayivaru halaruvaru dakṣenaḍuva
bhatta[ḷi] ·avarakayali

bāurāuttaru malavakoṁḍu tāu bādu nanage koṭu nānu bādu
baṁda kuduregaḍeya gaḍiya vivara

mūḍalu tirumeṭṭijaḍina gaḍiyiṁdaṁ paḍuvalu teṁkalu hoḷeya
gaḍiyiṁdaṁ baḍagalu paḍu-

valu hoḷe salama[ḍi]da vonakeya gaḍiyiṁdaṁ muḍalu baḍagalu
sālagaḍiyiṁdaṁ teṁkalu

yiṁttī nāku catusīmeya vaḷagaṇa sadraśa bittaṇakkemuḍi 16 gade
yigaḍi[ya] voḷagaṇa biṭṭu teṭṭu ·a-

sabhasa sabha[ma]ṁdu marapala hoḷekaḷu saha sābādu bidastaḷada meḷa haṭṭi
aṁgaḍiya lōkanāthadē-

vara ·ayana mūrudinada raṁgapūjege nāga[ṁ]ḍugadalu muḍi 4 ||
akkiyanu kālakālaṁ pratiya pratiyalū na-

ḍasi baha neṁdu koṭṭa dharma sadhanada patrayastaḷada ·āguhōgu dēvara
dēyastaḷake terubēḍige yila

yi patrake ·āga ·arike hoṇagaṇappa seṭṭimaga bayaraṇa seṭi saṣi doregaḷu
yisaraseṭi magatiṁ-

maya seṭi muguvina nāraṇaseṭṭi maga tirumaṇa seṭṭi ka • taravopu
hoṇigarana vopu talaura bra-

maru doṟigaḷa vopu yiṁtiyavara ·ubhaya vuttu[di]ṁda hoḍe madhestara
barahā [||*]

inscription.head:
No. 428.
(A.R. No. 566 of 1929-30.)
HATTIYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 4) set up in prākāra of the Lokanāthēśvara temple.

This registers a gift of land to god Lōkanātha of Haṭi-Añgaḍi by
the dēśa-samasta of Hāḍu-nāḍu for conducting the tuḍubina-utsava. The
gift land, situated at Bāḷāḍi, was made over to the god through Rāmā-
bhaṭṭa. Sēnabōva Kaseṭṭi wrote the record.

It is dated Śaka 1[5]02, Pramādhi, Vaiśākha ba. 10, Wednesday
corresponding to 1579 A.D., May 20.

inscription.head:
No. 429.
(A.R. No. 234 of 1931-32.)
GUṆḌUGALLU, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock in a field called ‘Sēshappa-maḍi’.

This badly damaged record refers to Bhīkshāvṛitti-ayya and seems
to register a gift the details of which cannot be made out.

It is dated the cyclic year Tāraṇa, Māgha ba. 14, Friday. On
palaeographical grounds the record may be assigned to the 16th century
in which case the details regularly correspond to 1584 A.D., January
31 which might be the intended date.

inscription.head:
No. 430.
(A.R. No. 567 of 1929-30.)
HATTYAṄGAḌI, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 4) set up in the prākāra of Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

This registers a gift of land at Hosabeṭṭu in Guḷavāḍi village in
Hār-nāḍu, by Baṅkiyarasa alias Hoṁneya-Kaṁbaḷi-voḍeya, to god
Lōkanāthadēva of Haṭṭiyaṅgaḍi.

It is dated Śaka 1512, Vikṛiti, Māgha śu. 3, Monday corresponding
to 1591 A.D., June 18.

inscription.head:
No. 431.
(A.R. No. 75 of 1939-40.)
MĀRŪRU, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken Slab (No. 2) set up behind the Pārśvanātha basti.

This is dated Śaka 1520, Hēmaḷaṁbi, Pushya śu. 2, Friday
corresponding to 1597 A.D., December 30. The Śaka year was current.

It registers a gift of land by Pārśvadēvi Binnāṇi for worship and
offering to Mēgananele Chadranātha, Ananta tīrthakara, Chavis-tīrthakara
and other deities.

inscription.head:
No. 432.
(A.R. No. 74 of 1939-40.)
MĀRŪRU, KARKAL TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Broken Slab set up in front of the Pārśvanātha-basti.

This registers a gift of land by Pārśvadēvi Binnāṇi sister of
Pāṇḍyapparasa Binnāṇi and others for the worship and offerings to
Pārśvanātha, Ananta-tīrthakara, Chavis-tīrthakara and other deities.

It is dated Śaka 1520, Viḷaṁbi, Chaitra śu. 3, Thursday,
Rohiṇi-nakshatra, corresponding to 1598 A.D., March 30. Thursday,
f.d.n. ·07. The tithi was śu. 4. Cf. No. 431 above.

inscription.head:
No. 433.
(A.R. No. 479 of 1928-29.)
BALAMAÑCHI, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Two Slabs built into a wall in the east prākāra of the Anantēśvara
temple.

This damaged and undated record registers gifts of land to the
temple of Baḷamañjadēva for worship and feeding Brāhmaṇas. It
mentions Tāyaṇa.

inscription.head:
No. 434.
(A.R. No. 572 of 1928-29.)
KANNARAPĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Pillar near the doorway of the central shrine of Durgā
Paramēśvarī temple.

This damaged record refers to the wife to Saṇkara-keṇṇārama
and the extant portion refers to the month of Paushya. The purport is
not clear.

inscription.head:
No. 435.
(A.R. No. 575 of 1929-30.)
AMBALPĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Slab (No. 2) set up by the side of the verandah in the prākāra of the
Janārdanaśvāmin temple.

This record is fragmentary as the beginning is completely effaced
and the slab is built in. It refers to gifts of land.

inscription.head:
No. 436.
(A.R. No. 271 of 1931-32.)
HOSĀḶA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up Yeḍatāru-seṭṭi's hāḍi (jungle)

This badly damaged record seems to register gifts of lands by
several seṭṭis. The details are all lost. On the other side of the slab is
engraved. No. G. N. 437 above.

inscription.head:
No. 437.
(A.R. No. 276-A of 1931-32.)
HOSĀḶA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab lying in the garden adjoining the tank at Chauḷikēri.

This badly damaged record registers some gifts of land, the
details of which are however lost. The extant portion refers to the
date śu. 15, Wednesday, the other details of which are also lost.

TEXTThis is engraved below No. 436 above. It has not been noticed in the
of the year. OBVERSE.

inscription.head:
No. 438.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1933-34.)
KĀPPALLE, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: East wall of the Lōkanāthēśvara temple.

It is dated the cyclic year Nandana, Āśvija śu. 1. These details
are insufficient for verification.

It seems to register a gift to the blacksmith Habōja, son of
Pāpōja probably by the carpenter Nala Timmōja, son of Aṅkōja. The
record is badly damaged and the details are, therefore, lost.

inscription.head:
No. 439.
(A.R. No. 160 of 1933-34.)
CHITTĀMŪR, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Base of the mānastaṁbha in the Jaina temple.

It states that Buśrēshṭhī of Mahā Ñāgakula had the mānastaṁbha
erected. The record is in Sanskrit.

The Tamil version (A. R. No. 517 of 1937-38) engraved on another
face of this mānastaṁbha states that this was the gift of Buśśeṭṭi, son
of Bāyi-śeṭṭi, a vaiśya of Jagatāṇi Gutti.

inscription.head:
No. 440.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1938-39.)
ŚRĪRAṄGAM, TRICHINOPOLY TALUK, TRICHINOPOLY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: South wall of the second prākāra of Raṅganātha temple.

It records the obeisance of Basavaya of Giṁḍi, the ūḷiga (servant)
of Kṛishṇarāya.

inscription.head:
No. 441.
(A.R. No. 323 of 1931-32.)
NAKKABAṆḌA, (NEAR KUMARANATTAM) PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR
DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of the Jaṅgaṁmaṭha.

This badly damaged record seems to register an endowment made
by Mummaḍi, Chikkarāya, son of Immaḍi Chikkarāya of Suguṭūru, to
his religious preceptor. The details of the grant cannot be made out.

It is dated the cyclic year Rudhirōdgāri, Jyēshṭha śu. 1[0],
[Wednesday]. On palaeographical grounds the record may be assigned
to the 17th century, in which case the details of date of the record
may probably correspond to 1623 A.D., May 28, Wednesday, f.d.t. ·25.

inscription.head:
No. 442.
(A.R. No. 64 of 1935-36.)
VIṬLA, PUTTUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Bali-pīṭha of the Pañchaliṅgēśvara temple.

It states that the bali-pīṭha in the temple of Pañchaliṅgadēva was
a gift of Mañjaṇṇa-seṭṭi of Kukke.

It is dated the cyclic year Pramōdūta, Vriśchika 1, Sunday. On
palaegraphical grounds the record may be assigned to the 17th century,
and the details equated to 1630 A.D., October 31.

l.ka:
svastiśrīmatu pramōdūta saṁvatsarada vriścika māsa 1 ·ādilu
paṁcaliṁgadēvara

saṁnidhiyalu kā[ḍa]raṁbaḷiya kukke maṁjaṇnaseṭṭimāḍsidaṁthā balisīṭhada
dharma maṁ-

inscription.head:
No. 443.
(A.R. No. 325 of 1931-32.)
SURĀLA, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone built into the Hirē-balikallu in the Mahāliṇgēśvara temple.

It states that the balikallu is a gift to the temple of Mahāliṇgadēva
of Surāla by a person (name not clear) who probably attachaed to the
bhaṇḍāra (treasury) of Chineya arasa Toḷaha.

It is dated Śaka 1566 [Tā]raṇa, Kārttika śu., the tithi being lost.
The date falls somtime in October-November of 1644, A.D.,

l.ka:
svasti śrījayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakavaruśa 1566 neya [tā]raṇa
saṁ kārtika [śuddha] • • •

• • śrīmatu dēvadēvōttama śrīsurāla māhāliṁgadēvara dēvatā
[dha]rmaśrīmatu sakalagu-

inscription.head:
No. 444.
(A.R. No. 266 of 1937-38.)
PEDDANAPĀḌU, KAMALAPURAM TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in front of the Āñjanēya temple.

The record states that Naraharidēva, the sthānapati of (the temple
of ?) Rāmachandrarāya, had the image of Hanumantadēva consecrated
at Peddanapāḍu.

It is dated the cyclic year Manmatha, Phālguṇa śu. 5,
[Wednesday]. On palaeographical grounds the record may be assigned
to the 17th century in which case the details correspond to 1656 A.D.,
February 20 which might be the date intended.

inscription.head:
No. 445.
(A.R. No. 72 of 1943-44.)
SAṆḌŪR, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab (No. 5) lying near the P.W.D. office.

This badly damaged record in characters of about the 17th
century seems to register a gift to god Mūlasthāna Mallikārjuna
probably by the gavuḍa, sēnabōva and others.

It is dated the cyclic year Rudhirōdgār in, Āśvija śu. [3,
Monday], probably corresponding to 1683 A.D., September 10, Monday
f.d.t. ·60.

inscription.head:
No. 446.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1936-37.)
KĀNDALVĀḌI, TIRUKKOYILUR TALUK, SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Wall of the Āñjanēya shrine.

This record is dated the cyclic year Pārthiva, Āśvija ba. 7. The
details are insufficient for verification.

It states that the garuḍa-gaṁbha was the gift of Kēshavaṇṇa,
grandson of Chaṇḍikeya Narasayya, to god Lakshmīnārāyaṇadēva of
Kāndalvāḍi.

Another record engraved below this, also dated in the cyclic year
Pārthiva, Āśvija ba., states that [Giriya]pa, son of Kaḷasayigaḷa
Timmapa had the temple of Laksmīnārāyaṇadēva constructed, at
Narāṇapura (Nārāyaṇapura).

inscription.head:
No. 447.
(A.R. No. 272 of 1936-37.)
TSADUM, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in the compound of Liṅgāyata-maṭha.

It is registers a gift of the village Vīrasamudra in the vaḷita of
Chadumu to god Mallikārjunadēva by daḷavāvi Virēgauḍa of Anekal,
with the permission of Chikkarāya-Tammegauḍa of Suguṭūru.

It is dated the cyclic year Rākshasa Āśvija śu. 10. The date
cannot be verified for want of further details.

inscription.head:
No. 448.
(A.R. No. 69 of 1943-44.)
SAṆḌŪR, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. 2) lying near the P.W.D. Office.

The record registers a gift of land as sarvamānya to Śarabhayya,
son of Rāghappayya by Ja[mnagiri] Bāva, the guru of Hindurāya.

It is dated Śaka 1625, Subhānu, Vaiśākha śu. 12, Sunday
corresponding to 1703 A.D., April, 16, Friday, f.d.t. ·65.

inscription.head:
No. 449.
(A.R. No. 67 of 1943-44.)
SAṆḌŪR, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. 3) lying in the compound of the local court.

This damaged record registers a gift of land in Chikke Soṇḍūr to
Jamunāgiri Bāvā by Hire Hindūrāya.

It is dated Śaka 1630, Sarvadhāri, Chaitra śu. 1 corresponding
to 1708 A.D., March, 12 The details cannot, however, be verified.

inscription.head:
No. 450.
(A.R. No. 66 of 1943-44.)
SAṆḌŪR, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone (No. 2) lying in the compound of the local court.

This is dated Śaka 1670, Vibhva, [Vaiśākha] śu. 10, Sunday.
These details are irregular as the tithi corresponds to 1748 A.D., April
27 when the weekday was Wednesday.

It states that the village Sāṇḍūru was given as Vumiḷigi to
Jamunāgiri-svāmi and that the latters samādhi was built by Giriyamma.
The record is written both in Kannaḍa and Telugu languages.

inscription.head:
No. 451.
(A.R. No. 511 of 1929-30.)
SANKAGIRI, TIRUCHCHENGODU TALUK, SALEM DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Step of the entrance into the central shrine of
Channakēśava temple on the hill.

This badly damaged record in modern characters refers to a
certain Chenaya.

inscription.head:
No. 452.
(A.R. No. 532 of 1929-30.)
BAINDŪRU, COONDAPOOR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Slab set up in front of the Baṅkēśvara temple.

This indifferently engraved record in late characters is badly
damaged and only the imprecatory portion can be made out.

inscription.head:
No. 453.
(A.R. No. 570 of 1929-30.)
KUTTUPĀḌI, UDIPI TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone set up in the prākāra of Māṅgōḍu Subbarāya temple.

The record is very badly damaged. It refers to the installation of
Śanidēva in the maṭha built by Nārāyaṇa-pādirāya, grandson of Halaṇa-
senabōva and mentions the jagattu and the village Kutupāḍi.

It is dated cyclic year Vikāri, Mīna 13, Monday, other details
being lost. It is in modern characters.

l.ka: śrīgu[ru*] vē gati [|*] śrīsarasvatyai namaḥ [|*] śrī • [ga]ṇē

inscription.head:
No. 454.
(A.R. No. 618 of 1929-30.)
SAṄKARABAṆḌA, PATTIKONDA TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock in front of a ruined shrine near the stream in the village.

This late record, dated the cyclic year Chitrabhānu, Vaiśākha
śu. 10, registers a gift of land by Aṅki-nāyaka to Namavāḍa
Timaraśaya.

inscription.head:
No. 455.
(A.R. No. 556 of 1931-32.)
TAMARĀJAPALLE, (NEAR VANAMALADINNE), PUNGANUR TALUK,
CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock to the south of the Ōṭiguṇṭa tank.

This damaged record in late characters seems to refer to the
construction of a tank and the grant of land to Tammarāya by
Komarōbaḷi-nāyaka. Chidōja appears to have been the engraver of the
record.

It is dated the cyclic year Bhāva, Vaiśākha śu. 15, details are
insufficient for verification.

inscription.head:
No. 456.
(A.R. No. 220 of 1931-32.)
NALLŪRUPALLE, (HAMLET OF BAṆḌLAPALLE), PUNGANUR TALUK,
CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone in the sluice of the tank.

This record, in late characters, refers to the allotment of six
portions of produce of the land below the tank to god Agatinata
(Agastya-nātha) and the remaining one portion to Taṁbiseṭi who
constructed the tank at Nalluru-haḷi under the direction of Timmaya
Samarasa what was the karaṇika of the temple. The allotment was
made by the latter.

It is dated the cyclic year Plavaṅga, Chaitra śu. 5; the details
are insufficient for verification.

l.note: The last line is built

inscription.head:
No. 457.
(A.R. No. 359 of 1931-32.)
MUTTUKŪRU, PUNGANUR TALUK, CHITTOOR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Two pieces of a slab lying in a field about a fur-long to the south-
east of the village.

This fragmentary record refers to a gift of land, the details of
which are all lost.

inscription.head:
No. 458.
(A.R. No. 452 of 1940-41.)
KONAKOṆḌLA, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Boulder on the hillock called the Rasāsiddhula-guṭṭa to the north of
the village.

This damaged record in late characters, refers to Śrī Vidyānandasvāmi
and Chippagiri. It is dated the cyclic year Dundubi, Pushya śu. 3; The
details are insufficient for verification.

inscription.head:
No. 459.
(A.R. No. 453 of 1940-41.)
KONAKOṆḌLA, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Boulder on the hillock called the Rasāsiddhula-guṭṭa to the north of
the village.

This badly damaged record in late characters, refers to a person
whose name seems to commence with Nāga.

inscription.head:
No. 460.
(A.R. No. 457 of 1940-41.)
KONAKOṆḌLA, GOOTY TALUK, ANANTAPUR DISTRICT.
inscription.p:
Boulder on the hillock called the Rasāsiddhula-guṭṭa to the north of
the village.

This record in late characters, refers to Bramadēva son of
Gummisseṭi. It appears to be incomplete.

inscription.head:
No. 461.
(A.R. No. 27 of 1943-44.)
KṚISHṆAPURAM, NANDIKOTKUR TALUK, KURNOOL DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Pillar (No. 2) in the maṇḍapa of the Śiva temple.

This record in late characters, is dated the cyclic year Tāraṇa,
Pushya ba. 3 . •; the details are insufficient for verification.

It records the obeisance of Vīraṇanāyaka son of Arasapa-nāyaka,
to god Mallikārjuna.

inscription.head:
No. 462.
(A.R. No. 65 of 1943-44.)
SAṆḌŪR, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying in the compound of the local court.

This record in late characters, is dated the cyclic year Vikāri,
Kārttika; other details are not given.

It registers a gift of land situated in Hosahaḷi, which was being
enjoyed as an uṁbaḷi village, by the donor Subhānaji Hindūrāvu
Ghōrapaḍe and his mother Guṇābāyi, to Kariyaṇṇa of Kṛishṇāpura. as
sarvamānya.

inscription.head:
No. 463.
(A.R. No. 82 of 1944-45.)
JAYASIṄGHAPŪR, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone placed on the parapet wall of the Māruti temple.

This damaged record, in late characters, refers to god Hanumappa
and to Hosama[le]. The other details are all lost.

inscription.head:
No. 464.
(A.R. No. 86 of 1944-45.)
SAṄKARĀPURA ALIAS GAṆḌI-BASĀPŪR, SANDUR TALUK,
BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Rock about 20 yards in front of the temple of Gaṇḍi-Basappa.

This damaged record, in late characters, seems to register a gift
for offering in the Bhikshā-ṁātḥa of Tiruveṅgaḍa-dēva of Raṅga-
samudra.

It is dated the cyclic year Durmati, Mārgaśira śu. 1; the details
are insufficient for verification.

l.ka: śrīguruvē gati [|*] durmati saṁvacarada mārgasira su-
dhdha 1 lu raṁgasamudrada tiruveṁ[ga] ḍa • dēva bhikṣāmaṭha ne-

inscription.head:
No. 465.
(A.R. No. 88 of 1944-45.)
TĀRANAGIRI, SANDUR TALUK, BELLARY DISTRICT.
inscription.p: Stone lying near the Buḍḍekallu.

This record, in late characters is fragmentary and too badly
damaged to yield any details.
